programme.title: Edge of Alaska

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Sage Halford seizes pit bulls that were allegedly used for fighting, and goes on to tackle their owner in court

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia feels overwhelmed by work, so she hires three new parolees to help out, only for things to take a turn for the worse - just as the shelter is swamped by a group of new dogs

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: The Safari Park team release a group of burrowing owls into the wild. A hamadryas baboon searches for a mate, but the keepers may need to help

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.sub-title: New Day for an Old Tortoise
programme.desc: Tortoise Jaws is unwell, so the staff work to bring him back to full health. Two giraffe calves need to be hand-reared and a koala is ready for a mate

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: The wardens are on high alert at the start of oyster season - and soon come across one crew with a large number of the molluscs. An owl is in need of rescue

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: The team heads to Romania to help with an out-of-control street dog problem. They also treat an injured European bison and try to restore a dogs hearing

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: A friend asks Pete Nelson to build him a treehouse strictly for fun and relaxation, so he creates a space complete with a wine bar, loft and even a hot tub

programme.title: Rugged Justice
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the Washington Department of Fish and Wildlife Law Enforcement Programme, as they police the Evergreen States unforgiving mountain terrain, twisted rainforest back roads and densely populated coastal areas

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The Kilchers prepare for the winter, and Otto clashes with Charlotte while building a staircase. Meanwhile, Eivin and Eve welcome the newest member of the family

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil starts the first Motherlode tour, but complications could bring his work to an end. Jeremy embarks on a bear hunt, but encounters something even more dangerous

programme.title: Deadly Islands
programme.desc: Biologist Dave Salmoni ventures to an island in the Galapagos archipelago in search of some of the weirdest and toughest animals on the planet

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: Expert frontiersman Marty Raney and his daughter Misty assist two Montana homesteaders who face being trapped on their property - only for a medical emergency to complicate matters

programme.title: The Pool Master
programme.desc: Anthony Archer-Wills visits some of the most luxurious pools in the world, from a $2million private oasis in the Las Vegas desert and the largest wave pool to a Moroccan-style palace in the heart of Houston

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Out on a Limb
programme.desc: Pete Nelson designs a 1,000sq ft Texan man cave treehouse, complete with its own 600lb custom meat smoker and water-gun turrets to fend off the girls

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: An undercover investigation leads the team to the shocking discovery of eight dogs tied to metal chains, dog-fighting paraphernalia, illegal drugs and a gun

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Andy hates girlfriend Alyssas demon cat Tosh, who turned after he moved in with her. Eric and Kaotars problem cat, Vincent, tries anything to escape

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: Two rehabilitated sloths are released back into the wild, the team reveals the athletic secrets of the jungle gym, and the resident scientist inspects a sloth skeleton

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: A tracking system is tested by sanctuary favourite Sidwiggy, the nursery manager becomes an anger management counsellor, and the scientist discusses sloth fur

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: A 57,000-gallon tank for a church is completed, and a female customer requests a keg tank from the brothers-in-law as a surprise gift for her fiance

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Volcanos have long helped shape the Earth, but what is less well known is that there are volcanos on other planets and moons that are even more extraordinary than those on our own home planet. Cameras follow a team of volcanologists in Iceland as they draw parallels with the volcanos on Earth and those elsewhere in the solar system

programme.title: An Art Lovers Guide
programme.desc: Historian Janina Ramirez and art critic Alastair Sooke embark on cultural city breaks, offering insights into the most famous locations and uncovering hidden gems and untold stories to reveal how religion, revolution and trailblazing individuals can shape the art - and soul - of a metropolis. They begin by examining the art and design of the Dutch capital Amsterdam, along the way discovering how its culture reflects the liberal attitudes, appetite for global trade and love of home comforts that helped shape the character of this city

programme.title: Tutankhamun: The Truth Uncovered
programme.desc: What killed Tutankhamun? Ever since the tomb of the boy king was discovered in 1922, he has become arguably the most famous pharaoh of all ancient Egypt. But his mysterious death at just 19 years of age has never been explained. Now, Dallas Campbell reveals new scientific research aimed at getting to the truth. A CT scan of the pharaohs mummified body creates a full-size image of the real Tutankhamun, while DNA analysis uncovers a shocking secret about his family background - and the genetic trail leads to a revolutionary new theory about his sudden and unexpected death

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: Artists and experts reveal how, during the reign of Elizabeth I, Protestants and Catholics used art, language and new technology to wage a battle for power in the Isles, creating surprising and often radical works. Author Stephanie Merritt re-assesses the John Foxes grisly Book of Martyrs as a work of history and nationalist propaganda, with passages performed by actress Morfydd Clark

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the evening session on day four from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the conclusion of Shaun Murphy and Judd Trumps respective first-round matches against qualifiers. Former champions Murphy and Trump claimed 10-4 wins against Daniel Wells and Zhou Yuelong at this stage last year to set up a meeting against each other. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Part two of two. Delving into the heart of the culture wars and freedom of speech conversations that have exploded on US campuses over the last ten years. Hearing from academics, activists and students on all sides of the divisive debates, while painting a picture of an educational environment marked by fear and threats

programme.title: Britains Nuclear Secrets: Inside Sellafield
programme.desc: Physicist Jim Al-Khalili explores the history of the nuclear facility near the village of Seascale in Cumbria, investigating Britains attempts - past, present and future - to harness the power of the atom. He examines the ways waste and spent fuel rods have been stored there over the past 70 years and looks at the latest attempts to try to clean some of it up, from storage in vast open-air ponds to encasing pieces of old reactors in concrete blocks

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Volcanos have long helped shape the Earth, but what is less well known is that there are volcanos on other planets and moons that are even more extraordinary than those on our own home planet. Cameras follow a team of volcanologists in Iceland as they draw parallels with the volcanos on Earth and those elsewhere in the solar system

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: A profile of the 20th-century escapologist, featuring restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists, who recall his impact on audiences. Plus, revealing some of the secrets behind some of Houdinis most famous escapes. Originally broadcast in 2000

programme.title: Britains Nuclear Secrets: Inside Sellafield
programme.desc: Physicist Jim Al-Khalili explores the history of the nuclear facility near the village of Seascale in Cumbria, investigating Britains attempts - past, present and future - to harness the power of the atom. He examines the ways waste and spent fuel rods have been stored there over the past 70 years and looks at the latest attempts to try to clean some of it up, from storage in vast open-air ponds to encasing pieces of old reactors in concrete blocks

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents coverage of the evening session on day five from the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the start of Neil Robertsons first-round match against a qualifier and the conclusion of Mark Selbys opener. Robertson and Selby were two of the highest profile opening-round casualties last year, losing to Chris Wakelin and Ben Woollaston respectively. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Anna finds herself forced to defend a dealer who sold her drugs, while Milly feels hurt and confused at being given the cold shoulder by ODonnell. With Amita Dhiri and Daniela Nardini

programme.title: Concorde: A Supersonic Story
programme.desc: As new £19million museum Aerospace Bristol prepares to open, this documentary takes a look at the life of the most glamorous plane ever built. Rare footage tells the forgotten row between the French and British governments over the name of Concorde, which threatened to derail the whole project. Engineers, flight technicians and frequent fliers tell the supersonic story, aided by politician Michael Heseltine and actress Joan Collins, while a former passenger recalls sharing an intimate moment with the Rolling Stones. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Shark
programme.desc: A look at how scientists are challenging traditional perceptions of sharks, with breakthroughs regarding the creatures intelligence and social skills presenting them in a surprising new light. The programme also highlights some researchers beliefs that studying the predators could help to solve some of the most pressing medical challenges of the 21st century. Narrated by Paul McGann

programme.title: Why the Industrial Revolution Happened Here
programme.desc: Jeremy Black examines one of the most extraordinary periods in English history, explaining the unique economic, social and political conditions that led to the nation becoming the richest and most powerful on Earth by the 19th century. He traces the unprecedented explosion of new ideas and technological inventions that transformed an agrarian society into an increasingly industrial and urbanised one

programme.title: Concorde: A Supersonic Story
programme.desc: As new £19million museum Aerospace Bristol prepares to open, this documentary takes a look at the life of the most glamorous plane ever built. Rare footage tells the forgotten row between the French and British governments over the name of Concorde, which threatened to derail the whole project. Engineers, flight technicians and frequent fliers tell the supersonic story, aided by politician Michael Heseltine and actress Joan Collins, while a former passenger recalls sharing an intimate moment with the Rolling Stones. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Surrey man Bobby Williams brings in the powered wheelchair that opened up a whole new sporting career for him as a youngster, allowing him to play the fiercely competitive game of powerchair football. Now moving into coaching, he wants to pass the chair on to a new player - but it needs restoration. The other items are a speedway flag that tells a remarkable Glaswegian love story, meticulously handcrafted jewellery and a bagatelle board

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones and Helen Skelton present the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: The disappearance of a father-of -four from Comber, Northern Ireland. Police suspected hed come to harm, but struggled to find proof without a body. Six weeks later, they discovered his body stuffed in a wheelie bin and dumped in a lake. They soon had two prime suspects, but detectives needed to find a way to place them and the victim at the same place at the same time, so obtained the services of a cell site analyst to track the suspects movements. Plus, how a soil expert unearthed the truth when a 63-year-old grandfather disappeared in the Highlands

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell meets an old friend in County Durham who is determined not to learn from his previous mistakes. Martin Roberts is pleased with the area hes in, just maybe not the property. Tommy Walsh thinks a demolition derby might be in order in Kent

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is in Kingston in Surrey, where the teams are led by Thomas Forrester and Gary Pe. While the competition hots up, Eric gets on his bike at the Herne Hill Velodrome

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in the Somerset town of Midsomer Norton, where couple Polly and Callum compete to raise funds for Macmillan, with the help of husband-and-wife experts Pete and Lola. With £100 and 60 minutes on the clock, they huunt for high-end labels and vintage glass, with Polly finding a steal in designer footwear, while Callum gambles on a dressing table

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: To launch a new spirit distilled on the cheese farm where he lives, Banjo Beale is asked to design a rustic gin-tasting bar using salvaged materials found on the farm. This design is deeply personal journey for Banjo, as he aims to give back to the people who welcomed him and his husband Ro into their community, but with a launch party looming, and an ambitious plan to complete, will it live up to expectations?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from a busy seaside resort dream of a new life in Somerset for their young family, with outside space for chickens, llamas and maybe even a pig. Denise Nurse takes on their countryside search, lining up a selection of homes, including one with spectacular views across the Bristol Channel to Wales. She also meets a creative pair who have already made their rural escape and gets hands on with some screen printing

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and his team of mentors help metal worker Diane from Northumberland and signwriter Sam from London turn their passions into businesses

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. Maria, Paul, Said, Max and Albert battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: A mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat, presented by Alex Jones and Roman Kemp

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ravi is determined to keep his family together. Mark tries to help Lauren. Elaine and Ian attend a gala

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and interiors guru Michelle Ogundehin welcome a new group of wannabe designers to the Brighton Design Studio, where each week they are challenged to transform a selection of vastly different spaces. They begin on the coast in Dorset, where they must transform basic wooden beach huts into stylish spaces which also show off their signature styles - while also taking into account sustainability. To judge their efforts, Michelle is joined by leading designer Jonathan Adler

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Restaurant critic Grace Dent and chef Anna Haugh take the reins as the cookery competition returns, beginning with six amateur cooks, including an IT teacher, a financial adviser and a construction company boss, competing for a coveted apron. They begin by producing their signature dish, before following a recipe from which key information is missing and cooking for three diners who know a thing or two about MasterChef - last years winner Harry Maguire and runners-up Claire Syrenne and Sophie Sugrue

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral (1994)
programme.desc: A London mans misfortunes in love look set to change when he meets an American beauty. However, the prospect of a transatlantic relationship succeeding seems slim, so the pair go their separate ways - only for him to realise he is unable to get her out of his mind. Richard Curtiss comedy, with Hugh Grant, Andie MacDowell, John Hannah, Charlotte Coleman, Kristin Scott Thomas, Simon Callow and James Fleet

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones and Helen Skelton present the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: Police turn to a forensic linguist to crack the code when the members of a violent gang use a secret language to discuss a fatal attack. Plus, one of the biggest cannabis-growing operations ever seen in the UK is brought down with the help of a drugs expert

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell meets a man in Carnoustie, Angus, who is renovating a home for him, his dog and a menagerie of other animals to live in. Dion Dublin decides it is not safe for him to enter a house in Merseyside and Jacqui Joseph finds theres space to play with in an Essex auction home

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams search for money-making items in Llandeilo, Carmarthenshire, with the help of Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson. Presenter Christina Trevanion heads to Laugharne to visit the home of writer Dylan Thomas

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in the West Yorkshire town of Hebden Bridge, where friends Emma and Sophie compete to raise money for Dove House Hospice, picking up items including a vintage St Michael jacket, a rare red rotary dial telephone, a 1970s Holly Hobbie doll and a colourful 1960s picnic set

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Interior designer Banjo Beale and his team of tradespeople travel across the islands, using their skills in a series of makeovers. Banjo starts out with the Puffer Pub on the tiny island of Easdale. Jhennia and Rachel quit their London life and moved to the island in 2022. However, raising a family and running a business has left them no time to renew the pubs old, tired interior. Can the designer and his team come up with a finish thats both new and in keeping with the pubs unique charm?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple from Wolverhampton to find a new home and lifestyle in Shropshire, before braving an outdoors adventure playground with a giant zip wire

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: With a love of colourful fashion and a passion for accessories, Londoner Tiana has been running her own crochet business for several years. However, sales have slumped, and she wants mentor Jeanette to determine where shes going wrong. In Lancashire, Potter Pip feels shes facing the last roll of the dice to create a successful ceramics business, so turns to mentor Stuart for advice. Presented by Dominic Chinea

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host as Giorgio, Katie, Caroline, Ben and John take their turn in the moving podiums, with correct answers getting the players moving and dictating how quickly they reach the finish line. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Vernon Kay present the magazine show, with celebrity guests and stories of interest from around the UK

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki starts to worry about the family. Max and Cindy grow closer. The Mitchells rally together

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Brothers Iain and Kirk Russell from Glasgow present Suzie Fletcher with a leather football made famous by their grandfather, Rangers player Ian McMillan. It was used in the Scottish Cup final replay between Rangers and fierce local rivals Celtic back in 1963, a game that Rangers won 3-0. Bookbinder Chris Shaw is asked to restore a magazine written by the owners father when he was being held in a Japanese PoW camp, Geoffrey Harvey restores a pachinko machine - a pinball-style device - for a pair of sisters, and Rebecca Houghton works on a lyra - a bowled folk instrument - from Crete

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Grace Dent and Anna Haugh put six more amateurs, including a tattoo artist, a flight operations controller and a retired US diplomat, through their paces in the MasterChef kitchen, asking each one to serve up their signature dish and follow a recipe that has key information missing. Those who make it through the first two rounds cook once more, before the next two quarter-finalists are decided

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth II: Her Story, Our Century
programme.desc: Documentary exploring a century of change in Britain through the prism of Queen Elizabeth IIs life. Through archive and eyewitness reflections, the film explores how she led the monarchy through profound social, political and cultural change. With contributions from Helen Mirren, David Attenborough, Barack Obama, Tony Blair, and HM Queen Camilla

programme.title: Celebrity Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents a celebrity edition of the high stakes quiz, with Amy Dowden, Nina Wadia, Montell Douglas and Toyah Willcox playing to win money for their chosen charities. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the comedy quiz show in which a panel of six celebrities fill in the missing blanks and help win some brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This week, he is joined by Rhod Gilbert, Jill Scott, Aisling Bea, Guz Khan, Nitro and the Rev Kate Bottley

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Gethin Jones and Louise Minchin present the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Short film about four very different schoolgirls thrown together as they plan their escape from detention. Written by and starring Sabrina Ali

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: As a new mother, Staceys worries for the future - and about death - have only exacerbated. In order to try to overcome her fears, she wants to face death head on, so joins the team of funeral directors at Nottinghamshire-based firm, AW Lymn, one of the largest independent funeral enterprises in the country. Stacey fully immerses herself in every aspect of the companys day-to-day work to discover more about the business of death - from arranging funerals to making coffins, learning more about embalming to carving elaborate headstones - and tries to get the answers she has been searching for

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Frances second-largest city Marseille has been the nations drug capital for over 50 years, but the violence of its drug war is escalating, as rival gangs kidnap, torture, kill and even set one another on fire. Ashley spends time in the notorious northern suburbs known as the cites, meeting young men involved in fighting the bloody drug war as well as riding along with the police on a night-time patrol

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Gabon in search of crocodiles, chimpanzees and feisty elephants

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Bali for a diving adventure to find a giant ocean sunfish

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams bid farewell to Europe and head into Asia, racing from bustling Istanbu to the remote town of Halfeti in south-east Turkey - where the last to arrive will be eliminated from the competition. Along the way, Katie persuades brother Harrison to tick off a bucket list experience - watching the hot-air balloons rise over the chimneys of Cappadocia - while the leaders in the last leg, cousins Puja and Roshni, continue to celebrate, this time because its Pujas birthday

programme.title: Better Date than Never
programme.desc: A group of people take their first steps into the dating scene, much later in life than is typical. Olivia cant wait to head out on her first ever date, and Charles is excited to meet his match, but Nirvali struggles with nerves as she prepares for a blind date

programme.title: Better Date than Never
programme.desc: Dianne is ready to find love as her authentic self, Charles gets guidance from a relationship expert, and Nirvali braves her first date as Olivia moves on to her second

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britains most maligned ethnic minorities. In Bedfordshire she meets 23-year-old Chantelle, who prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her grandmother taught her and shares them on social media, where she has nearly 400,000 TikTok followers. In Lincolnshire, the reporter meets 18-year-old Serena, who is planning her wedding to her fiance Marco. He is also from a Gypsy and Traveller community and Serena wouldnt have it any other way

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand this apparent high-street epidemic, Stacey heads to Manchester to meet the people behind the crime and spends time on the shop floor with the staff trying to prevent further thefts

programme.title: The Royal Hotel (2023)
programme.desc: American best friends Hanna and Liv run out of money in Sydney as they backpack around Australia. The cash-strapped twentysomethings seek temporary employment to pay for the rest of the trip. However, after taking a job in a pub, they are confronted by the unruly locals. Thriller, starring Julia Garner, Jessica Henwick, Hugo Weaving and Ursula Yovich

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to the Philippines to find out if joining a gang is the only way to survive in the slum districts of the capital Manila. From street kids with few choices to families making homes among the graves of the citys notorious North Cemetery, Ashley discovers how gang life has changed in the Philippines and what factors still drive young men to join them

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: The former footballer visits some of the worlds most dangerous cities to explore the issues that affect young men born into a life on the fringes of society. He begins in the favelas of Rio, Brazil, where he learns what it takes to become a lookout, foot soldier and drug dealer in a world where death could become reality at any moment. He also comes face-to-face with a gun-toting militia boss to uncover how these men rule through fear and intimidation

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: South Africa has for years battled the scourge of rhino poaching, fuelled by insatiable demand for their horns in Asia. Ashley embarks on a mission to find out who is behind these acts, discovering how the challenges young men face growing up in deprived rural communities can lead them into poaching

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Frances second-largest city Marseille has been the nations drug capital for over 50 years, but the violence of its drug war is escalating, as rival gangs kidnap, torture, kill and even set one another on fire. Ashley spends time in the notorious northern suburbs known as the cites, meeting young men involved in fighting the bloody drug war as well as riding along with the police on a night-time patrol

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve witnesses a serval hunting in the weird surroundings of a power station at night, puts snakes to the test in a chomp-off and meets a rare spider

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Norway to dive with one of the planets ultimate predators - the orca. Steve also takes a look at herring, which are one of the orcas favourite fish

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan and interiors guru Michelle Ogundehin welcome a new group of wannabe designers to the Brighton Design Studio, where each week they are challenged to transform a selection of vastly different spaces. They begin on the coast in Dorset, where they must transform basic wooden beach huts into stylish spaces which also show off their signature styles - while also taking into account sustainability. To judge their efforts, Michelle is joined by leading designer Jonathan Adler

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its the final and as usual the remaining two candidates go head to head to win Lord Sugars investment. Supported by a handful of returning candidates, they must launch their businesses to a roomful of industry and a few familiar faces from the past 20 years, before the business mogul decides who will become his next partner

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: The former footballer heads deep into the Colombian jungle to find out why gold is now twice as valuable than cocaine to the countrys organised crime groups, meeting the young men drawn into this illegal venture, discovering the shocking health impact mining is having on them, as well as meeting the cartel gangsters who control almost every element of life in these remote regions

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to Sweden where he joins police at the front line of the ongoing gang war and meets with gang bosses in underground car parks. As trouble expands throughout the country, children as young as 13 are taking weapons and justice into their own hands, a photojournalist shows him their documentation of the murder scenes before he meets with a top gang leader for a training session involving pistol target practice in the dead of night

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part one of two. The journalist and media personality meets four young women who have reported being raped by someone they know. With unprecedented access to women who have waived their right to anonymity after reporting being raped, Stacey spends three years following the complainants in the lead-up to trial. She explores the challenges of reporting rape when the alleged perpetrator is someone familiar, and asks whether the justice system delivers what those desperate to be believed hope for

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britains most maligned ethnic minorities. In Bedfordshire she meets 23-year-old Chantelle, who prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her grandmother taught her and shares them on social media, where she has nearly 400,000 TikTok followers. In Lincolnshire, the reporter meets 18-year-old Serena, who is planning her wedding to her fiance Marco. He is also from a Gypsy and Traveller community and Serena wouldnt have it any other way

programme.title: My Mates a Bad Date
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Khalil from Nottingham cannot get a second date. Oloni thinks that remembering his dates names, dropping the baby girl tag and cooling off on the bragging might help his chances

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part one of two. The journalist and media personality meets four young women who have reported being raped by someone they know. With unprecedented access to women who have waived their right to anonymity after reporting being raped, Stacey spends three years following the complainants in the lead-up to trial. She explores the challenges of reporting rape when the alleged perpetrator is someone familiar, and asks whether the justice system delivers what those desperate to be believed hope for

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Stacey continues to follow the emotional journey of the young women who have reported rape and now trying to navigate the justice system, she learns to appreciate that the statistics are stark. Fewer than two per cent of reported sexual assaults result in conviction, though this increases to over 50 per cent for cases that proceed to trial. As their trials near, the women learn that credibility also plays a pivotal role

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Catrin Heledd presents extended highlights of a first-round match from day four at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith head to the National Forest, to mark the planting of its 10-millionth tree. They discover how three decades of regeneration have transformed scarred mining land into a rich wildlife habitat, and meet people whose lives have been reshaped by the Forest, from a womens walking group to craftspeople building sustainable livelihoods from woodland materials. In Devon, Adam Henson meets a young farming couple reviving the practice of traditional woodland grazing

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: In the early hours a frantic teenage girl calls 999 to say that her friend has been attacked and stabbed in a car park. Police rush to the scene, where they find a 16-year-old with a life-threatening wound to his stomach. While his life hangs in the balance, West Midlands Polices homicide team get to work, with forensic investigators discovering two discarded weapons at the scene - a kitchen knife and a machete

programme.title: Race Across the World

programme.sub-title: High Stakes GPS Sting and a Life Saved by Air Ambulance
programme.desc: Police close in on an organised crime gang targeting farms across northern England and stealing high-tech GPS systems. After months of painstaking detective work, a high stakes sting operation is launched to bring the thieves to justice. In Leicestershire, a huge barn fire devastates a family run dairy farm, threatening cattle and destroying winter feed. In Scotland, reporter Paul Colgan meets farmer Debbie Duffus, as she is reunited with the air ambulance team who saved her life

Wakelin was a quarter-finalist last year, and 2019 champion Trump went one better, going out in the semi-finals. programme.title: Talking Snooker
programme.desc: Alistair McGowan looks at the legends of the green baize who helped make snooker both a national obsession and a TV phenomenon, uncovering interviews with players who set new standards in the game. He takes viewers from the explosion of characters and colour in the 1970s and the dominance of Steve Davis and Stephen Hendry to the famous black-ball final of 1985 between Davis and Dennis Taylor - a game that shattered nerves and TV viewing figures

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.desc: After three months at sea, the test pilots from the Royal Navy and RAF have successfully landed the F35 jets on the flight deck of HMS Queen Elizabeth for the first time, and even tested launching and landing in hurricane conditions. But with just a few weeks to go before the warship arrives in New York in spectacular style, they are about to try a method of landing never attempted before, which carries great risks but could revolutionise carrier operations globally. Narrated by Caroline Catz

programme.desc: Brandon and Lori are confused by a 3D printer, but are delighted with the valuation, while Papa Bear bids on two mysterious lots from Brazil

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Kidderminster, Worcestershire, where antiques dealer Alison Chapman falls in love with a sweetheart brooch, Henry Nicholls does all he can to secure some silver cap badges and a military watch and James Layte examines a set of 1930s curtains

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts visit Wilmslow, Cheshire, where they help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. Items under consideration include a Victorian board game, a Wedgwood tea set and a puppet of movie star Charlie Chaplin

programme.title: Gold Wars Downunder
programme.desc: Three hardcore mining teams battle it out on the one patch of gold-rich-land at the town of Clermont to see who can unearth the biggest nuggets and the most gold. There are nine weeks left of the gold season to go and the pressure is on. At Swiss Diggings, an unemployed Donnie begs owner Dennis for a new job

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Ashley and Kaylyn charge onto Bear Ridge. Zak and Bruce answer an urgent call for help, while Troy and Tes go into battle to defend a bird sanctuary. Plus, Dusty puts on his inventor hat to save the Glades

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: Aerial cameras reveal strange aquatic mammals populating a river in Columbia, a ship marooned in a sea of sand along Namibias notorious Skeleton Coast, a Boeing 727 jetliner hidden in a pine forest. Plus, a giant, unusual pattern is discovered in the Nevada desert

programme.title: UFOs: Secret Missions Exposed
programme.desc: Man has always been captivated with outer space. This film considers whether it is possible that government has been hiding top-secret space missions to the moon and Mars

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists ask whether a currently unidentified species represented in human DNA could be a result of breeding between man and alien in the distant past

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: A circle of wild turkeys parade around the body of a dead cat in an area known for witchcraft, while ghostly figures stalk the site of a bloody Civil War battlefield. A mysterious blue blaze is like nothing firefighters have had to deal with before near Yellowstone

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A customer wants to sell a coat allegedly made of rare monkey fur. Seth is tempted to make a purchase that could prove beneficial to the finances of the business

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The toss of a coin determines the value of a gold chain Les believes is out of fashion. A customer is upset when Seth prevents her from using the staff toilets

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A woman rejects custom-made earrings, as she thought the term diamond cut meant they were diamonds. Seth puts his faith into younger sellers, believing they can outperform Les

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson travels to Coalville in Leicestershire, with dealers James Layte, Tony Geering, Alison Chapman and Ian Towning. Among the items brought in are an art nouveau stamp box, a veterinary kit from the 1800s, and a watch bracelet that once belonged to Diana Dors

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts visit Wilmslow, Cheshire, where they help members of the public sort the treasures from their trash. Items under consideration include a Victorian board game, a Wedgwood tea set and a puppet of movie star Charlie Chaplin

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A seller brings a Mr T wall clock to the shop, Rick checks out James Bonds credit card from the 2006 movie Casino Royale, while Corey plays with an Atomic Cannon toy. Chum and Rick get down and dirty in Texas looking for fossil

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Father-and-son duo Paul and Bogart go head to head in Claireville, Toronto, with each hoping to claim the bigger profit

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.sub-title: Big Cow Trouble in Little Ajax
programme.desc: The grand opening of Paul and Bogarts store finally arrives, but their plans suffer a setback when a new buyer shows up on the scene with money to burn

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Piles to Go Before I Keep
programme.desc: Laura takes the reigns alone in Fullerton and proceeds to outsell Dan. Brandi gets assaulted by her locker but ultimately drives home a winner. Rene postpones a day date in favour of a work date, while Darrell stumbles across and unexpected collection

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: In Victorville, a friendly duel runs Dusty up, though his mother lode of vinyl might just save him. Ivys locker shines some light on the business, while Lisa learns that she needs her locker about as much as she needs a hole in her head

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori win a surfboard, while Jesse and Papa Bear team up to bid on a safe protected by barbed wire

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Lori goes behind Brandons back as she teams up with Jesse, while Papa Bear thinks there might be a shotgun hidden in one lot

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Cameras capture mysterious red lights shooting into the sky above the Alps, prompting scientists to try to recreate the phenomenon with an experiment. A pack of dogs rips a car apart, leaving experts searching for some possible motive

programme.title: Alien Corridors
programme.desc: There are recordings of possible UFO and UAP sightings across the world which have created patterns. This series investigates whether the Earth is being mapped by intergalactic forces. A team of multi-disciplined experts - believers and critics - investigates what these Alien Corridors are and what they mean. Washington State holds the United States highest rates of UFO sightings per capita. With a new report made every 55 hours, why are UFOs choosing to hover here?

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.sub-title: Over and Out of This World
programme.desc: Ranch owner Brandon Fugal and his team, led by chief investigator Erik Bard and astrophysicist, aerospace engineer and optical scientist Dr Travis Taylor elevate their investigation of the mysterious 512-acre Utah property as a technologist scans the ranch while hanging out of a helicopter

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick sees red when a Rolex Submariner hits the counter, Corey gets slap-happy over a hockey stick signed by Paul Newman, while Chum and Santos are sweet on a set of Mr Softee trading cards. Later, Rick heads to the range to see a set of Bolivian thunder mugs

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: How the West Covina Was Won

programme.title: The Bidding Room

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Cold Case Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The inside story of the cold case investigation into the murder of an 86-year-old who was stabbed multiple times in 2013, before her body was set on fire. More than a decade later, detectives from the Major Crime Unit decide to reopen the investigation hoping that advances in DNA science will mean they can finally identify the killer

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britains best-known personalities appraise recent TV shows including Squid Game, Glastonbury, Open House: The Great Sex Experiment, Celebrity Bridge of Lies, Trainwreck: Poop Cruise, Millionaire Hoarders and Gary Barlows Food and Wine Tour: Australia

programme.title: Grayson Perry Has Seen the Future
programme.desc: Artist Grayson Perry heads for California on a mission to understand how Silicon Valleys booming tech industries are likely to shape the human future. His two-part journey begins in the Bay Area where he is assisted by an increasingly chummy chatbot. He meets everyone from leading players in the industry to idealistic young start-up founders, as well as ordinary people who are adopting the latest innovations and alarmed critics of the direction AI is taking the world

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to family owned Italian restaurant Giuseppis in Macomb Township, Michigan. Father and son Joe and Sam Borgias constant fighting is driving customers away, and Gordon discovers one of the restaurateurs is concealing a serious health problem. Nevertheless, he sets about rescuing them from disaster and turning around the failing business

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a man trying to build the first UFO-styled prefabricated Futuro house seen in Britain for 50 years, and a woman creating a magical garden room using centuries-old techniques, involving earth, sand, straw and even human hair. Georges Italian road trip takes him to a traditional village, where he discovers the most modern of houses with an extraordinary design featuring a floating swimming pool. And, as he and Will get down to work on their wilderness retreat, theres a close shave involving an oak tree

programme.title: Britains Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: John from the country office has a £3.6 million Arts and Crafts-style south coast seaside mansion to sell, Polina is listing a new central London penthouse worth £4.5 million - so new that it is not even on the market yet - and company owner Guy is in Barbados to view a $40 million beachside property where the rich and famous have stayed. Amber is keen to win one of Londons most expensive properties, a £75 million Knightsbridge home that once belonged to prima ballerina Margot Fonteyn

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Japan is often portrayed as a modern utopia, but beneath its polished exterior, one of the worlds oldest populations is reaching a breaking point. As traditional multi-generational families break down, millions of elderly people are being left in isolation and poverty - with many turning to petty theft to survive. As Japanese prisons become retirement homes for a growing number of elderly convicts, Krishnan Guru-Murthy investigates this geriatric crime wave gripping the country

programme.title: Kirsties House of Craft
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp takes on food-inspired crafts by creating vegetable-printed greeting cards and trinket dishes made from salt dough

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: As Frankie prepares to say goodbye to the village, the Keane clan pack up their belongings, ready to surprise their estranged family by moving to Hollyoaks. But in a devastating twist of fate, everything spirals out of control, and its a race against time when an overturned van is spotted on the side of the road... Meanwhile, a residents buried secret is revealed. And while things look up for Dodger and Theresa, Kathleen-Angel causes tension. Writers: Emily Gascoyne, Steve Hughes; Dir: Sean Glynn; Prod Co: Lime PicturesShort: As Frankie prepares to leave Hollyoaks, the Keane family get ready to move in. But in a devastating twist of fate, things spiral out of control. And a buried secret is revealed.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Richard Whitehead in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm decides to make some major changes in his life after a chest X-ray reveals a worrying spot - but he does not seem ready to start living like a saint. Bar-room comedy, starring George Wendt and Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam is seduced by a friend of Diane who knows what she wants and how to get it. American bar-room comedy, guest starring Carol Kane, with Ted Danson and Shelley Long

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Frasier is disillusioned to discover a colleague has fallen head over heels in love with Carla. Comedy, starring Ted Danson, Kelsey Grammer and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of two. Flashback episode in which the Barones recall their courtship and wedding, beginning with Rays nervous marriage proposal - and subsequent uncertainty over Debras motive for accepting

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Barones continue reminiscing about their courtship and wedding, recalling Rays marriage proposal and the subsequent uncertainty over Debras motive for accepting. Afraid she may have felt pressured, he decides to give her a second opportunity to reject him

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: The Barones are forced to stay with Rays parents while their home is fumigated, providing Debra with the perfect opportunity to turn the tables by interfering in Frank and Maries lives. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A forthcoming squash tournament has Frasier on the lookout for a new partner, and an attractive gym coach seems to fit the bill. When lust blooms, the teachers aggressive performance techniques cannot help but remind the psychiatrist of his old gym teacher. Comedy, guest starring Jeanne Tripplehorn and Bob Hoskins, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphnes mother infuriates Martin by meddling in his romantic affairs, while Frasier and Niles take part in a charity cycling event - even though neither of them knows how to ride a bike

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin dispenses advice to a depressed Kenny, leaving Frasier feeling jealous, and a yoga class goes badly for Niles, who is taken aback to discover he is not as supple as Daphne. Guest starring Alan Cumming

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Crane brothers are delighted when their social standing grows thanks to a Russian caviar dealer, who offers them good quality roe at knock-down prices. Daphne tries to persuade a reluctant Martin to return $40 dispensed in error by a cash machine

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The acerbic chef tries to transform six-month-old New Jersey gastropub Bask 46, which is in jeopardy due to the antics of its equally fiery head chef

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien visit Leicestershire, where they meet Bernadette, whose uncle Jim has filled every room with motorbikes, cycles and various other things

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers explore Northumberlands Ford and Etal estates, where they meet a flock of rare four-horned Hebridean sheep and help maintain one of the countrys few working watermills. Janice hitches a lift in a sidecar during a vintage motorcycle run, and the family gets up close and personal with the residents at one of the north of Englands largest stables of heavy horses

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Paralympian Richard Whitehead in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is house hunting with John and Debbie around the Mar Menor on the Costa Calida in south-east Spain. The couple are dreaming of a two-bedroom holiday home thats near the beach. They have a budget of £80,000 to buy an apartment or townhouse, which could become their permanent home in the future. But can Lucy find them the perfect property?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Couple Rob and Steve launch new events at their French vineyard in Gironde in attempts to keep up with rising production costs and after a huge renovation project, husband-and-wife team Nick and Jenny get to run their Bergerac-based BandB at full capacity. Gary and Lynne suffer a shock departure at their padel tennis club on the Costa Blanca

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Sasha race to transform their courtyard in time for impending back-to-back weddings. A crumbling roof threatens Anna and Edwards deluxe wine weekend, while Roger and Louisa desperately need a new en-suite

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sophie and Damon Rodriguez help Chichester retirees Jill and Dene find the perfect balance of comfort and outdoor living in Alicante. In Murcia, Darren and Natalie Brown hunt for a spacious villa for Brighton couple Paul and Bobbie and their family

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: The last space to be finished in Keith and Marjs living quarters is the entrance lobby connecting the main chapel hall to the living space and the soon-to-be pottery studio. The plan is to fit the space out with a clash of styles, so Marj refurbishes an old metal locker, while Keith mounts antlers to bring some baronial flair

programme.title: Suez: 24 Hours That Broke the British Empire
programme.desc: Part two of two. The inside story of November 6, 1956, the final day of the Suez Crisis. British troops are in Egypt supposedly on a peace-keeping mission, to protect the Suez Canal. But all is not as it seems. Nobody knows it, but the next 24 hours will change Britain for ever and determine the future shape of the world, told hour by hour, minute by minute through the eyes of historians and those at the heart of Government

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordons late-night investigation of Mrs Whites Golden Rule Cafe turns up shocking results, and a bombshell reveals a secret financial crisis. Will the 101-year-old proprietor and her 75-year-old son Larry ever hand over the reins and retire?

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 50-year-old woman is rushed to AandE having collapsed at work following a thunderclap headache, leaving doctors concerned that she may have a bleed on her brain. Her husband shares the story of how they met and the challenges they have had to overcome. Other patients include a 13-year-old boy hit by a car while riding his bike and a woman concerned by a headache, who describes her childhood spent in Zambia and Lancashire

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Cold Case Murder
programme.desc: The second of a two-part special looking into the murder of 86-year-old Una Crown, who was stabbed multiple times in 2013. Forensics get a partial DNA match to a man who had previously been arrested but released due to lack of evidence. Police decide to bring him in once more in the hope that after 13 years of denial, he will finally confess to the killing

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: After a fall down the stairs leaves painter and decorator Mick with a possible bleed on the brain, the Thames Valley Air Ambulance crew take over his breathing to give him the best chance of survival. The Essex and Herts Air Ambulance team administer ketamine to Don, a motorcyclist with a broken leg and severely deformed wrist. Plus, Doreen needs help after being trampled by a foal

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a restaurant in Mount Sinai, New York, which appears to be stuck in the 1980s. The crumbling decor and unenthusiastic head chef at the Handlebar need to be tackled, but owners Bill and Carolyn LeRoy are in denial about the problems they face

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan and Lydia West. After not seeing each other for a year, Maggie and Eddie are reunited as bridesmaids at a mutual friends wedding. Maggie is dead set on showing everyone how stable she is, only to be given a junior bridesmaid status by the scary maid of honour. Eddie, meanwhile, is intent on cutting ties with Maggie for good. But first, she must team up with her estranged friend to save the wedding from a very unwelcome guest

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Maggie and Eddie discover their favourite drag act are performing for one night only - and tickets are like gold dust. Thus begins an odyssey across London to get tickets, involving wigs, theatrical icons, lies and a whole lot of drama. Maggie is desperate to remind Eddie how much fun they used to have, but Eddies new friend Whitney is determined to keep Eddie focused on getting a job to fund their future plans. Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan, Lydia West and Hannah Onslow, with a guest appearance from Rupert Everett

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Greater Manchester compete to win the £1,000 prize, including Amy and Matt, who kick off the competition with a varied menu that causes chaos in their kitchen. The second dinner party of the week sees Paula and Andy prepare a selection of family favourites, but Sharons husband Simon raises a few eyebrows when he makes a frank admission at the dinner table

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George visits a Victorian villa in Leeds, West Yorkshire, where Jawad and Charlie are planning a master en-suite which, if the rest of their quirky renovations are anything to go by, looks set to be a mammoth task. He also helps Jason and Hannah in Brixton, south London, who are tackling an ambitious two-storey extension. The big question is - should they lose their redundant chimney breast and increase the size of the room?

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Paralympian Richard Whitehead in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: The Heart Is a Lonely Snipe Hunter
programme.desc: At Dianes request, the guys take Frasier hunting in the woods - but end up sneaking off and abandoning him miles from anywhere. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane struggles to understand why Sam has agreed to take part in a charity softball game - until she finds out the opposing team is made up of glamorous Playboy Playmates. Ted Danson and Shelley Long star

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam joins Coach at night school to get his diploma - and soon arouses Dianes suspicions when he begins to do unusually well. Comedy, with Ted Danson and Nicholas Colasanto

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank backs into Roberts patrol car, prompting Debra to ban him from driving the kids anywhere - and leaving Ray contemplating his dads advancing years. American comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra hires a babysitter - whose subsequent popularity with the children leaves her quietly seething with jealousy. To make matters worse, Marie feels insulted because she was not offered the job. Comedy, starring Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray fears Debra will seek revenge after he embarrasses her at a school auction. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink turns interior designer to help a local home-building scheme, but his plans prove too grand for the new residents, and he is soon shown the door. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A street singer annoys Frasier and Niles, prompting them to set out in search of a new coffee house to frequent, and Daphne has her physical therapy techniques evaluated by an independent professional. Elvis Costello guest stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin is given the night shift as punishment for refusing to take his bosss sister out on a date, and Frasier becomes jealous of his former agent Bebes close relationship with a celebrity lifestyle guru. Guest starring Dr Phil McGraw

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin worries he may not be Niles and Frasiers father after noticing their similarities to his wifes former research partner. Meanwhile, Daphne enrols her yet-to-be-conceived child at an exclusive school. Comedy, guest starring David Ogden Stiers

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon attempts to revamp an incohesive country club and golf course restaurant in New Jersey thats dark, gloomy, not attracting the golfers and receiving numerous complaints

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in Norfolk, where items include an old pedal cycle with a two-stroke engine attached and a side table made from a military shell case

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers head to Co Durhams Ushaw House, where theyre given an exclusive glimpse of a largely unknown collection, including priceless treasures buried with a saint, rare clothes from an ancient royal wardrobe and the spellbinding artistry of the buildings. They also help out in the grounds, immerse themselves in the sites annual 1940s day, and head out into the Durham Dales to help weigh baby alpacas on a nearby farm

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Paralympian Richard Whitehead in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King helps Jenny from Chingford, east London, buy a property in San Antonio on the Balearic island of Ibiza. With a £250,000 budget at her disposal, Jenny is looking for a two-bedroom apartment close to the beach. Coming along to help with the search is her good friend Baz, so Leah has to people to impress with the five options she lines up

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Nadine and Simon brainstorm ideas to expand their glamping business in France and hotelier Tanya searches for a new rural retreat in Spain. Joel and Cori give their all as they launch a new venture in the Spanish countryside

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: DIY novices Lionel and Claire tackle a luxury en-suite. Alison and Zion deal with gaping ceilings as they rebuild a top-floor apartment. Tim and Sasha prepare for not one but two huge weddings

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, the Garners help Alison and John find the perfect villa for their new Spanish life. On the Costa Blanca, the Aldertons show Hollywood casting director Nancy a sleek, ultra-modern home that tests her taste

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: On the Cornish coast, Danny and Danielle take on a multimillion-pound mansion with spectacular views - and spectacular problems. A storm batters the sea wall, emergency repairs mount up and before work can even begin, they must find £40,000 to rehome the resident bats. In north Wales, the clock is ticking for Aled and Sarah. They want to move in together before their wedding in just a few months, but with the renovation funded by their monthly income, theyre doing the hard graft themselves

programme.title: Grayson Perry Has Seen the Future
programme.desc: Grayson concludes his tour of Silicon Valley by considering the motivations of the men behind the technology, such as Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos - and how their values might be challenged. He meets a scientist working on empathetic robots which offer a more human-centric alternative to the priorities of the industry and encounters one of the key players in AI, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark, grappling with the possibility that AI could be shaped in such a way to offer a more optimistic future

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: The chefs thorough late-night investigation of MacGregors unearths far scarier secrets than the ghosts that are rumoured to haunt the restaurants dining room - mouldy leftovers, frozen produce and the previous owners discarded belongings. Burdened by the past, and up to her neck in debt, new owner Stephanie allows head chef Mike, whos also her boyfriend, to cut corners in the kitchen. Can Gordon orchestrate a fresh start?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The viewers check out Secret Garden, Love on the Spectrum, Celebrity Bridge of Lies, Gordon Ramsays Secret Service, The Housemaid and Conspiracies and Coverups

programme.title: Rescue: Extreme Medics
programme.desc: An air ambulance is dispatched to the island of Mull, where 23-year-old rally driver Scott has rolled his car down a 20-foot embankment. In Glasgow city centre, the trauma team fight to save the life of a man who was hit by a bus and dragged under the wheels, while the coast guard helicopter flies to a remote sea loch to rescue a woman who has fallen 15 feet down a waterfall

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Babylon in New York, where the peace of Long Island is regularly shattered by the volatile Pellegrino family, proprietors of Peters Italian Restaurant. Gordon finds owner Tinas work to save the eatery from oblivion is frequently ruined by the behaviour of her brother, a tyrannical front-of-house manager whose violent temper constantly undermines staff morale

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Comedian, writer and actor Asim Chaudhry joins Joe Lycett to spend a weekend in the French city, where they sign up for a skateboarding lesson in an abandoned tobacco factory that now serves as a hub for all things hipster. Other cultural highlights include the Tunnel of 1,000 Signs and a guided tour of the Underwater Museum, which comprises 10 sculptures submerged in the Mediterranean

programme.title: Like a Boss (2020)
programme.desc: Friends Mia and Mel run their own beauty company. However, things take a turn for the worse when their business runs into a massive amount of debt. They are forced to turn to ruthless benefactor Claire Luna, head of a major cosmetics empire, who intends to steal the business from under them. Comedy, starring Tiffany Haddish, Rose Byrne and Salma Hayek

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George is in Wirral to help retired couple Charles and Ian, who are finally getting round to a complete redesign of their Edwardian home. But will they agree to his radical changes or are they too set in their ways? George also tackles an 1850s country house in Surrey which has accrued some bizarre additions over the years, including a 1970s MDF staircase, a baronial fireplace and a random room that contains nothing but the fuse box

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three sets of dinner-party hosts from Nottingham compete, beginning with Ollie and his girlfriend Deb, who think their holiday-inspired menu has what it takes to win. On the second night, footballer Luke and his fiancee Nikki present their guests with a selection of Thai-themed treats, while the final soiree sees Jemima and Mark prepare dishes inspired by their many trips to Spain

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to the Isle of Wight to uncover four new items, and restorer Ronnie relives his past life on Ibiza via a three-wheeled trike

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Paralympian Richard Whitehead in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm offers to deliver a poorly Cliffs letters for him, but ends up getting arrested for tampering with the mail. George Wendt stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: An ambitious bet jeopardises Sams ownership of the bar when it transpires he has not met the deadline for marrying Jacqueline Bisset

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sams reputation as a ladies man takes a battering when his legendary charm fails to win over a female journalist whos investigating the singles scene

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debras mother Lois comes to stay with the family for a week, but seems to be more interested in her crossword puzzles than in Ray or the children. Katherine Helmond guest stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra decides it is Rays turn to take charge of birth control, but he backs out of having a vasectomy and buys a less drastic form of contraception - which Frank somehow mistakes for sweets. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert grows tired of the lack of privacy at his parents house and moves out, only to end up living with a couple who are uncannily similar to Marie and Frank

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz goes for a job interview at a rival station, and Frasier shares an impromptu kiss with Julia while trying to steal files from her exs office. Niles visits a shooting range and ends up enjoying the experience far more than anticipated. American comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, David Hyde Pierce and Peri Gilpin

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Cold Case Murder

Police decide to bring him in once more in the hope that after 13 years of denial, he will finally confess to the killing

programme.title: Grayson Perry Has Seen the Future
programme.desc: Artist Grayson Perry heads for California on a mission to understand how Silicon Valleys booming tech industries are likely to shape the human future. His two-part journey begins in the Bay Area where he is assisted by an increasingly chummy chatbot. He meets everyone from leading players in the industry to idealistic young start-up founders, as well as ordinary people who are adopting the latest innovations and alarmed critics of the direction AI is taking the world

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to family owned Italian restaurant Giuseppis in Macomb Township, Michigan. Father and son Joe and Sam Borgias constant fighting is driving customers away, and Gordon discovers one of the restaurateurs is concealing a serious health problem. Nevertheless, he sets about rescuing them from disaster and turning around the failing business

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a man trying to build the first UFO-styled prefabricated Futuro house seen in Britain for 50 years, and a woman creating a magical garden room using centuries-old techniques, involving earth, sand, straw and even human hair. Georges Italian road trip takes him to a traditional village, where he discovers the most modern of houses with an extraordinary design featuring a floating swimming pool. And, as he and Will get down to work on their wilderness retreat, theres a close shave involving an oak tree

programme.title: Britains Most Expensive Houses
