programme.desc: Sophie and Damon Rodriguez help Chichester retirees Jill and Dene find the perfect balance of comfort and outdoor living in Alicante. In Murcia, Darren and Natalie Brown hunt for a spacious villa for Brighton couple Paul and Bobbie and their family

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: The last space to be finished in Keith and Marjs living quarters is the entrance lobby connecting the main chapel hall to the living space and the soon-to-be pottery studio. The plan is to fit the space out with a clash of styles, so Marj refurbishes an old metal locker, while Keith mounts antlers to bring some baronial flair

programme.title: Suez: 24 Hours That Broke the British Empire
programme.desc: Part two of two. The inside story of November 6, 1956, the final day of the Suez Crisis. British troops are in Egypt supposedly on a peace-keeping mission, to protect the Suez Canal. But all is not as it seems. Nobody knows it, but the next 24 hours will change Britain for ever and determine the future shape of the world, told hour by hour, minute by minute through the eyes of historians and those at the heart of Government

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordons late-night investigation of Mrs Whites Golden Rule Cafe turns up shocking results, and a bombshell reveals a secret financial crisis. Will the 101-year-old proprietor and her 75-year-old son Larry ever hand over the reins and retire?

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.sub-title: 24 Hours in Police Custody: The Cold Case Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The inside story of the cold case investigation into the murder of an 86-year-old who was stabbed multiple times in 2013, before her body was set on fire. More than a decade later, detectives from the Major Crime Unit decide to reopen the investigation hoping that advances in DNA science will mean they can finally identify the killer

programme.title: Rescue: Extreme Medics
programme.desc: An air ambulance is dispatched to the island of Mull, where 23-year-old rally driver Scott has rolled his car down a 20-foot embankment. In Glasgow city centre, the trauma team fight to save the life of a man who was hit by a bus and dragged under the wheels, while the coast guard helicopter flies to a remote sea loch to rescue a woman who has fallen 15 feet down a waterfall

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Babylon in New York, where the peace of Long Island is regularly shattered by the volatile Pellegrino family, proprietors of Peters Italian Restaurant. Gordon finds owner Tinas work to save the eatery from oblivion is frequently ruined by the behaviour of her brother, a tyrannical front-of-house manager whose violent temper constantly undermines staff morale

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: After a fall down the stairs leaves painter and decorator Mick with a possible bleed on the brain, the Thames Valley Air Ambulance crew take over his breathing to give him the best chance of survival. The Essex and Herts Air Ambulance team administer ketamine to Don, a motorcyclist with a broken leg and severely deformed wrist. Plus, Doreen needs help after being trampled by a foal

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of a restaurant in Mount Sinai, New York, which appears to be stuck in the 1980s. The crumbling decor and unenthusiastic head chef at the Handlebar need to be tackled, but owners Bill and Carolyn LeRoy are in denial about the problems they face

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan and Lydia West. After not seeing each other for a year, Maggie and Eddie are reunited as bridesmaids at a mutual friends wedding. Maggie is dead set on showing everyone how stable she is, only to be given a junior bridesmaid status by the scary maid of honour. Eddie, meanwhile, is intent on cutting ties with Maggie for good. But first, she must team up with her estranged friend to save the wedding from a very unwelcome guest

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Maggie and Eddie discover their favourite drag act are performing for one night only - and tickets are like gold dust. Thus begins an odyssey across London to get tickets, involving wigs, theatrical icons, lies and a whole lot of drama. Maggie is desperate to remind Eddie how much fun they used to have, but Eddies new friend Whitney is determined to keep Eddie focused on getting a job to fund their future plans. Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan, Lydia West and Hannah Onslow, with a guest appearance from Rupert Everett

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Greater Manchester compete to win the £1,000 prize, including Amy and Matt, who kick off the competition with a varied menu that causes chaos in their kitchen. The second dinner party of the week sees Paula and Andy prepare a selection of family favourites, but Sharons husband Simon raises a few eyebrows when he makes a frank admission at the dinner table

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home

He also helps Jason and Hannah in Brixton, south London, who are tackling an ambitious two-storey extension. The big question is - should they lose their redundant chimney breast and increase the size of the room?

programme.desc: Diane struggles to understand why Sam has agreed to take part in a charity softball game - until she finds out the opposing team is made up of glamorous Playboy Playmates. Ted Danson and Shelley Long star

programme.desc: Frank backs into Roberts patrol car, prompting Debra to ban him from driving the kids anywhere - and leaving Ray contemplating his dads advancing years. American comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.desc: Ray fears Debra will seek revenge after he embarrasses her at a school auction. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.desc: The shrink turns interior designer to help a local home-building scheme, but his plans prove too grand for the new residents, and he is soon shown the door. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin is given the night shift as punishment for refusing to take his bosss sister out on a date, and Frasier becomes jealous of his former agent Bebes close relationship with a celebrity lifestyle guru. Guest starring Dr Phil McGraw

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin worries he may not be Niles and Frasiers father after noticing their similarities to his wifes former research partner. Meanwhile, Daphne enrols her yet-to-be-conceived child at an exclusive school. Comedy, guest starring David Ogden Stiers

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon attempts to revamp an incohesive country club and golf course restaurant in New Jersey thats dark, gloomy, not attracting the golfers and receiving numerous complaints

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in Norfolk, where items include an old pedal cycle with a two-stroke engine attached and a side table made from a military shell case

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers head to Co Durhams Ushaw House, where theyre given an exclusive glimpse of a largely unknown collection, including priceless treasures buried with a saint, rare clothes from an ancient royal wardrobe and the spellbinding artistry of the buildings. They also help out in the grounds, immerse themselves in the sites annual 1940s day, and head out into the Durham Dales to help weigh baby alpacas on a nearby farm

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Paralympian Richard Whitehead in Dictionary Corner

Coming along to help with the search is her good friend Baz, so Leah has to people to impress with the five options she lines up

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Nadine and Simon brainstorm ideas to expand their glamping business in France and hotelier Tanya searches for a new rural retreat in Spain. Joel and Cori give their all as they launch a new venture in the Spanish countryside

programme.title: Chateau DIY

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Help! I Bought It at Auction with Sarah Beeny
programme.desc: On the Cornish coast, Danny and Danielle take on a multimillion-pound mansion with spectacular views - and spectacular problems. A storm batters the sea wall, emergency repairs mount up and before work can even begin, they must find £40,000 to rehome the resident bats. In north Wales, the clock is ticking for Aled and Sarah. They want to move in together before their wedding in just a few months, but with the renovation funded by their monthly income, theyre doing the hard graft themselves

programme.title: Grayson Perry Has Seen the Future
programme.desc: Grayson concludes his tour of Silicon Valley by considering the motivations of the men behind the technology, such as Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos - and how their values might be challenged. He meets a scientist working on empathetic robots which offer a more human-centric alternative to the priorities of the industry and encounters one of the key players in AI, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark, grappling with the possibility that AI could be shaped in such a way to offer a more optimistic future

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: The chefs thorough late-night investigation of MacGregors unearths far scarier secrets than the ghosts that are rumoured to haunt the restaurants dining room - mouldy leftovers, frozen produce and the previous owners discarded belongings. Burdened by the past, and up to her neck in debt, new owner Stephanie allows head chef Mike, whos also her boyfriend, to cut corners in the kitchen. Can Gordon orchestrate a fresh start?

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A 50-year-old woman is rushed to AandE having collapsed at work following a thunderclap headache, leaving doctors concerned that she may have a bleed on her brain. Her husband shares the story of how they met and the challenges they have had to overcome. Other patients include a 13-year-old boy hit by a car while riding his bike and a woman concerned by a headache, who describes her childhood spent in Zambia and Lancashire

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In

Other cultural highlights include the Tunnel of 1,000 Signs and a guided tour of the Underwater Museum, which comprises 10 sculptures submerged in the Mediterranean

Comedy, starring Tiffany Haddish, Rose Byrne and Salma Hayek

programme.desc: Part two of two. As everyone makes their final preparations for Rosss wedding in London, Rachel tries to get a flight out to declare her love for him before he marries Emily. Guest starring Jennifer Saunders, Elliott Gould, Hugh Laurie and Tom Conti

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One After Ross Says Rachel
programme.desc: Despite Rosss massive blunder, he and Emily go through with the wedding - but she is clearly devastated and sneaks out of a window at the reception, declaring she never wants to see him again. Guest starring Helen Baxendale, Elliott Gould and Jennifer Saunders

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Kissing
programme.desc: Rachel returns from Greece and Chandler proves popular with the womenfolk. Phoebe feels upset about missing out on all the excitement in London, so the gang decides to treat her to a weekend in Atlantic City. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Surrogate mum Phoebe goes into labour and gives birth to triplets, but it proves harder than she anticipated to hand them over to her brother. Meanwhile, Joey experiences some pain of his own and Monica plans to spite Chandler by dating a male nurse

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Phoebe Hates PBS
programme.desc: Emily makes a request that could change Rosss life for ever, presenting him with a huge dilemma. Chandler upsets Monica by gloating about sex, while Joey challenges Phoebe to perform a completely selfless act. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer, Matthew Perry and Courteney Cox

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross delivers devastating news to Rachel - he cannot spend time with her while Emily is in New York. Meanwhile, Joey discovers Chandlers secret romance. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and David Schwimmer

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Emily asks Ross to move away from his friends, while Monica and Rachel are understandably taken aback to meet a hairy man who they mistake for a yeti. American comedy, starring David Schwimmer and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Moves In
programme.desc: Ross moves in with Chandler and Joey, but the transition does not go as smoothly as expected. Danny deals Rachels ego a blow by introducing her to an exceptionally dull date, and Phoebe becomes disillusioned with the health inspector she is seeing

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Thanksgivings
programme.desc: The pals digest a huge Thanksgiving meal and reminisce about their worst holiday experiences, including the time Chandlers father left his mother in favour of the houseboy, and the occasion in 1987 when a teenage Monica overheard Chandler calling her fat. Guest starring Morgan Fairchild

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey tries to cover up Chandler and Monicas relationship - but only succeeds in making himself look stupid. Phoebe persuades Rachel to take a literature class with her, while Ross falls out with his co-workers, convinced one of them stole his prized sandwich

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Inappropriate Sister
programme.desc: Ross struggles to come to terms with life as one of the unemployed, and turns to Joey for advice. Rachel is disturbed by her new boyfriends uncomfortably close relationship with his sister. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Resolutions
programme.desc: The pals announce resolutions for the coming year and Chandler enlists Joeys help so he can kiss Monica at midnight during a New Years Eve party. American comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Courtney Cox

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with Chandlers Work Laugh
programme.desc: Monica is annoyed by the fake laugh Chandler uses at work whenever his boss cracks a joke and the gang gets a shock when Ross introduces his new girlfriend

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe meets her biological father for the first time and Rachel helps Joey look a little more sophisticated with the addition of a trendy shoulder bag - which everyone else mistakes for a womens handbag

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Everybody Finds Out
programme.desc: Chandler and Monicas relationship finally becomes public knowledge to all but Ross, who is starting to get his life back on track by renting a flat opposite the girls. Comedy, starring Courteney Cox and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Girl Who Hits Joey
programme.desc: Ross makes a bad impression on his new neighbours, and Monica piles the pressure on Chandler, demanding more commitment than he feels able to give. Joeys latest girlfriend proves a little too fiery - but he is afraid to end their relationship

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor and his son hit the road together again, but this time they are staying closer to home and heading for Europe as they travel from Essex to Italys Amalfi coast. Their first stop is the Netherlands, where they try out the sport of canal jumping, before the pair don Lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion, and take on the worlds tallest freestanding climbing wall

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney continue their European adventure in Germany. They head to the city of Frankfurt, where Brad is pushed to the edge, literally, as he attempts to abseil down the side of a building, facing forwards. The boys also make a stop at a bratwurst-themed hotel and soak up some local Bavarian culture when they have a go at finger wrestling. The pair finish off their time in Germany with a wet and wild adventure in the mountains

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show
programme.desc: The lively comedian hosts an evening of entertainment, recorded in front of an audience assembled at Londons Theatre Royal on Drury Lane. Each week, Michael introduces sets by his fellow stand-ups, musical performances, dance revues and various other hijinks. He begins by inviting RandB star Tinie Tempah to perform, and the Peres Brothers Group put on a dazzling acrobatic show. The host also hijacks former Spice Girl Geri Horners mobile phone in Celebrity Send To All, which sees him fire of an embassassing message to all the contacts in her directory, and one unsuspecting member of the public is given a chance to sing a special duet with Michael Ball in Unexpected Star

programme.desc: Uzzi catapults Melissas tragic tatt into outer space, Alice removes Emmas fish-themed stinker, and Sketch gives Elliot a design to be proud of after losing his legs to meningitis

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets more tasks to Dara Ó Briain, Fern Brady, John Kearns, Munya Chawawa and Sarah Millican, the intrepid players hoping to become the 14th Taskmaster champion. As the quintet of cowering comics heads out into the wilderness, Alex Horne takes notes and records numbers with admirable - bordering on irritating - efficiency

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a hybrid of the comedy panel show and the words-and-numbers quiz, as Sarah Millican and Tom Allen take on Joe Wilkinson and Rosie Jones. Mathematician Rachel Riley looks after the letters and numbers, while Mr Swallow joins lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Recording artist Darius Rucker goes undercover to find Americas next great musical act. Darius first gained fame as lead vocalist and rhythm guitarist of rock band Hootie and the Blowfish, selling over 16 million records and winning two Grammys. He then went on to have a successful solo career as a county music artist. Now Darius wants to find talented but undiscovered new music acts and help launch their careers, so dons a disguise to work at an open mic night, as a roadie and a busker in his efforts to locate fresh songwriting and performing talent

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur Marcus Samuelsson goes undercover to find and mentor Americas next great chef. An Ethiopian refugee adopted by a Swedish family, Marcus developed a deep love for cooking at an early age and has since become a renowned force in international cuisine, with over 30 restaurants worldwide. Marcus disguises himself to work incognito in a teaching kitchen, a food truck business, a culinary institute and a soup kitchen, as he searches for undiscovered chefs to mentor

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: The DIY duo are back for series four! You know the drill: expect funky furnishings, hardware hysteria and, of course, problematic properties. Can Jonathan and Drew reverse more housing screw ups? David and Siobhan are ready to create a bidding war in their citys red-hot real estate. But first, they need the brothers to work their magic. What tricks do Jonathan and Drew have up their sleeves to secure a sale and land the couple their perfect property in one of the most expensive neighbourhoods in town?Short:David and Siobhan keep getting outbid in their citys intense market. What tricks do Jonathan and Drew have up their sleeves to secure a sale and land the couple a perfect property?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: City slicker Corey and his wife Angela kick off the series of dinner parties in Somerset at their grand Georgian home, serving up an Asian-themed menu inspired by their passion for travelling. Day two sees stay-at-home-dad Brian and his wife Kelly prepare traditional British dishes, but Corey criticises every course on the menu. Finally, outspoken Australian Shannon and her husband Tom host the third evening, hoping to satisfy hard-to-please Corey with their European cuisine, and win the cash prize

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Farm dwellers Nikki and Clive kick off the contest in East Anglia, serving up a menu of rustic fare. Self-confessed recluses, this is their first dinner party, so it remains to be seen how they cope having company round. They are followed by young couple Lloyd and Molly, who serve up a Thai menu, and bringing up the rear are Pete and Kirsty, with an evening themed around the letter P. Can they pocket the prize with perfect points? Its doubtful, but as ever itll be fun seeing how they do

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Fashionista groom-to-be James arranges a cutting-edge, catwalk fashion show ceremony full of the latest trends for his and his fiancees big day. However, bride-to-be Jenna is dreaming of a traditional and timeless wedding that will never go out of style

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: As the wedding planning show gets an upgrade, grooms-to-be are now given £14,000 to plan every detail of their nuptials - although they must still do so without any input from their significant others. The latest run gets under way by introducing Andrew, who hopes a big-top-themed wedding will distract his bride-to-be Jenni from the troubles she faces on a daily basis. Jennis confidence has taken a knock since she developed a disability caused by pregnancy, and while Andrew became her dutiful carer, he admits romance has taken a back-seat, and hopes an extravagant wedding will help him show his fiancee the spark is still there

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Walk-Ons is a sports bar chain with plans to double in size, but whether its 4,000 employees are ready to make this audacious move is unclear. Founder Brandon Landry and co-owner, NFL champion New Orleans Saints quarterback Drew Brees hope to find out by hiding in plain sight, joining staff on the front line in their restaurants. They meet a kitchen porter, an assistant manager and a dock supervisor for their main shrimp supplier, in a bid to get to the heart of their business. In the end, Brandon and Drew reveal their true identities and help make their employees dreams a reality.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Clean Harbours is a hazardous waste disposal company, dealing with everything from hurricanes and oil spills to the 9/11 clean-up operation. Its chairman and founder Alan McKim is struggling to avoid losing highly trained employees as the business expands, so he goes undercover as a mechanic to discover the reason why

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to New Orleans to visit Zekes, a much-loved restaurant that has seen standards slip since the original owner died. The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying and Selling
programme.desc: The DIY duo are back for series four! You know the drill: expect funky furnishings, hardware hysteria and, of course, problematic properties. programme.title: Peep Show

programme.desc: YouTube star and internet sensation Bethany Mota goes undercover to meet up-and-coming social media hopefuls who are trying to break through. programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: American football and baseball star Deion Sanders goes undercover to meet inspiring sports coaches and deserving fans. programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Action adventure, starring Robert Mitchum, Robert Wagner, Richard Egan, May Britt and Lee Philips

programme.desc: After the fall of Tobruk in 1942, an American tank crew face a desperate race against time to reach a source of water in an abandoned fortress - only to discover the Nazis are heading for the same location. Second World War drama, starring Humphrey Bogart, J Carrol Naish, Dan Duryea and Rex Ingram

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Part two of two. On trial for hijacking the Enterprise to take it to the planet of Talos IV, Spock produces evidence to prove Captain Pike made contact with the Talosians. Sci-fi adventure, starring Leonard Nimoy

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve is mystified when a sniper comes up with an airtight alibi for the attempted assassination of a government prosecutor. Gary Lockwood guest stars

programme.title: T.J. Hooker
programme.desc: A cocaine dealer offers to wipe out the debts of compulsive gamblers who owe money to his high-stakes poker club - but only if they agree to act as drug couriers. Vintage American drama, starring William Shatner

Thriller, starring Michael Caine, David Warner, Frank Middlemass, Don Henderson, Dudley Sutton and Colin Jeavons

Drama based on a true story, starring Dylan McDermott, Anthony Barrile and Michael P Boatman

programme.title: Split Second (1992)

programme.desc: A meek scientist becomes obsessed with his work, unaware he is driving his neglected wife into the arms of a conniving friend. The doctor develops a potion that unleashes his debauched alter ego, who throws himself into the debauched underworld of Victorian London before seeking violent revenge on those who have wronged them both. Hammer Horror, starring Paul Massie, Dawn Addams and Christopher Lee

However, he has to contend with the consequences when the actions of an arrogant hotshot pilot lead to the death of a fellow veteran airman. Drama, starring John Wayne, John Carroll, Anna Lee and Paul Kelly

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri (1954)
programme.desc: An American naval reserve pilot is called up for service in the Korean War, and must leave his family and civilian job as a lawyer. In command of a corps of naval pilots, he heads a bombing campaign on strategic Communist positions, but later comes to question the morality of the conflict. Adventure, starring William Holden, Grace Kelly, Fredric March and Mickey Rooney

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: Kirk laughs off suggestions that a respected actor is really the tyrant responsible for a massacre he saw as a child. However, a shocking discovery changes his mind and sets him off on a quest for the truth. William Shane and Arnold Moss star

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man
programme.desc: Steve gets into trouble trying to help Americas first female astronaut, Major Kelly Wood. Fantasy adventure, starring Lee Majors and Farrah Fawcett

programme.desc: Gage and Sydney infiltrate a seedy bar in an effort to track down Gages missing sister, while Walker and Alex leave town for a romantic weekend but are interrupted by cub scouts

programme.desc: A rebellious martial arts expert competes in an underground fighting circuit so he can raise money to save his temple. However, he risks corrupting his spirit as he is drawn further down the path of violence, while the organisers of the arena have their own sinister plans for him. Action adventure, starring Tiger Hu Chen and Keanu Reeves, who also directed. In Cantonese, Mandarin and English

programme.desc: A hard-up reformed crook agrees to pay off his debts by doing one last job for his psychotic former boss. Thriller, starring Andy Garcia, Christopher Walken, Treat Williams, Gabrielle Anwar and Steve Buscemi

programme.desc: A veteran motor racing driver is persuaded to come out of retirement, but ends up in direct competition with his own son, who has signed up with a rival team. Drama, starring John Travolta, Shania Twain and Michael Madsen

programme.title: The Six Million Dollar Man

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Ninety-one-year-old Cyril is airlifted to hospital after a car crash which required him to be cut from his car. Meanwhile, the medical team treat 57-year-old Caroline, who arrives at St Georges with chest pains, plus five-year-old Roman has a pencil stuck in his ear

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: A man suffers multiple injuries after a quad-bike accident on a farm near Penrith. Reported anaphylaxis following a bee sting has the heli-medics racing to the aid of Melody. Plus, cyclist Vivienne is in a bad way after her bike leaves the road on the notoriously steep Wrynose Pass in Cumbria

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old motorcyclist Sam is airlifted to St Georges after crashing into a bus and being thrown under a car. He is placed in an induced coma and CT scans reveal Sam has bruising to his lungs, a ruptured kidney, a broken thigh bone and a fractured spine. The priority of the medics is is to stop his internal bleeding, while Sams parents are rushed to the hospital by police. Meanwhile, seven-year-old Raheem arrives in AandE with his dad Dennis after cutting his wrist while playing at home, and 59-year-old Therese turns up with a painful lump on her finger

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Surgeons must plan their next move carefully when Daisy, a 22-month-old girl, arrives at St Georges Hospital by air ambulance after being kicked in the head by a horse at her familys stables. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mike, who has a form of medication-resistant epilepsy, arrives in minors with a bandage around his jaw after falling face-first onto the pavement. Plus, 100-year-old Florence, who was diagnosed with dementia three years earlier, and her daughter Frances arrive at the hospital when the former suffers what appears to be a stroke

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.sub-title: Supermarkets Unwrapped: Fruit and Veg Aisle
programme.desc: The team take their trolleys down the fruit and veg aisle. Kate Quilton is in Kenya to see how summer vegetables get to British supermarkets in the thick of winter. Jimmy Doherty looks at the measures taken to transport bananas across the UK, John Whaite finds out why supermarket blackberries are so big, Helen Lawal investigates a robot berry-picker in Portugal, and Matt Tebbutt finds out how we can waste less fruit and veg

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in Windsor, Berkshire, to meet Roy, whose garages are stacked full of bikes. Henry picks the one that doesnt work to take away, while Simon converts some 17th-century wooden beams into a pair of stylish bedside lamps

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of Guest and the City, Dan and Mardi Leonard, welcome their visitors in Brighton, but there is a mixed reaction to the couples contemporary decor and stained glass windows. The unconventional furniture leaves some guests confused, and one pair of fellow hoteliers bemoan the lack of sausages at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Phil Kiernan invites the contestants to the Farmers Boy Inn in Longhope, Gloucestershire, where he challenges them to a pie-making competition. However, the task proves less entertaining than the bickering that occurs between two guests as they try to share a kitchen

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Jane Asher host on the Geanna, their purpose-built hotel boat. Dan and Mardi Leonard are concerned space is too tight, while Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa worry about their privacy being invaded. A trip up the Thames allows the guests to sip champagne and get to know the ropes, but the mood at dinner is subdued, and when the owners cook their first breakfast on board they find themselves bickering

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa, who have run the Sentry Mead BandB on the Isle of Wight for six years, play host in the last challenge of the week. The guests arrive with high expectations and after a few setbacks, the dinner and breakfast win everyone over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the weeks competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business consultant James Herbert hosts the first dinner party in Lichfield, Staffordshire, and has high hopes that his guests - football coach Tim Travers, sales assistant JP Goodyear-Martinez, businesswoman Amanda Smith and retired mental-health nurse Ronnie Imlah - will enjoy his complex Italian menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired mental-health nurse Ronnie Imlah hosts the second dinner party in Lichfield, Staffordshire, hoping her menu inspired by dishes she has eaten at friends events will win her the £1,000. A revelation by the host leaves her companions in shock, and two guests fail to see eye-to-eye on plastic surgery

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: JP Goodyear-Martinez hosts the third dinner party in Lichfield, Staffordshire, where he treats his guests to a mix of Jamaican food, tight Lycra outfits and a drag queen. However, James Herberts rendition of Queens Bohemian Rhapsody does not go down too well with fellow diner Ronnie

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Football coach Tim Travers hosts the fourth dinner party in Lichfield, Staffordshire, and is determined to prove his culinary skills, despite never having cooked before. He tackles an American-themed menu with the help of his mother, but discovers the hardest task is keeping the peace at the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment consultant Amanda Smith hosts the final dinner party in Lichfield, and hopes to impress with a well-executed, sophisticated menu. However the night is not to everyone elses liking and an accident at the table leaves her guests in hysterics, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Old Stables, Somerset, where Finnish-born Nina Dodd and her husband Andy welcome guests to their Scandinavian-style accommodation. While some are impressed by the immaculate interior, David and Erica Hensley are not pleased about the no-shoes policy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Rigsbys Guest House in Hertfordshire, where owner Morris Cockman entertains his rivals with a visit to a local zoo. Not all of his guests are impressed, and a slow breakfast service does not improve his chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Erica Hensley host at the Fairway in Blackpool, where they hope to win over their guests with a spot of classic seaside entertainment. After taking their fellow contestants on a horror walk, the couple will be wary of any unexpected surprises when it comes to their scores

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Robin Hughes is the final host of the week in the converted Dent Station in the Yorkshire Dales. He organises a butchery competition and high-end food, before some home truths about the group dynamic are revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. Robin Hughes remarks from across the week are discussed, before the winner of the competition is revealed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil help househunters in Edinburgh. Phil meets Freya and Elliot who are moving from Aberdeen to the capital for the cultural buzz. Kirstie helps Edinburgh woman Sadia and Irishman Sohail find their first family home so they can finally move out of Sadias starter flat

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Gary and Bill in the Edinburgh suburb of Portobello, who are unsure whether to sell their 19th-century four-bed home or renovate instead. Bill loves their homes character, quirkiness and close proximity to the seaside, but Gary hates the busy road and the homes layout, and would like to settle down somewhere new

programme.title: Bill Baileys Australian Adventure
programme.desc: The comedian makes a journey across Western Australia. In the first episode, Bill encounters true-blue Aussie locals, animals and landscapes in Western Australias far south. He visits the best beach in the world and the spectacular trees of the Valley of the Giants; goes paddleboarding in the chilly Southern Ocean with a former NBA star, and discovers the early stages of a secessionist movement

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on peoples expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A young mother suffers a panic attack while out with her three-year-old son. When paramedics arrive, shes gasping for breath. A two-year-old whos running a high temperature and has a history of respiratory problems needs to go to hospital - and fast. A deaf woman has collapsed in her bedroom and the crew needs to find out why shes fallen, though she cant understand what theyre saying. A 999 caller reports a man lying unconscious at a bus stop, but finding the patient proves difficult. Plus, paramedics are called to a man whos recovering from knee surgery

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Cameras follow Lawrence, 26, who is rushed to St Georges after being hit by a car while crossing the road on a night out in Eastbourne. Meanwhile, 53-year-old Katrina is brought to AandE after having difficulty breathing and experiencing chest pains that could indicate a heart attack

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Hartlepool, the heli-medics treat a man whos been hit by a train. Cyclist Trevor suffers serious injuries after a collision with a car. Plus, in Cumbria, 12-year-old motocross biker Arai is flown across the country for emergency surgery on a deep chest wound

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Ninety-one-year-old Cyril is airlifted to hospital after a car crash which required him to be cut from his car. Meanwhile, the medical team treat 57-year-old Caroline, who arrives at St Georges with chest pains, plus five-year-old Roman has a pencil stuck in his ear

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.desc: The Food Unwrapped team explore the snacks aisle. Kate Quilton visits Kenya to find out how many mangoes are in a snack pack, Helen Lawal learns the mystery of the fluffy centre of a snowball, John Whaite cracks open the secret of caramel popcorn, and Andi Oliver investigates what a supermarket aisle means when youre shopping online

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo head to Herefordshire to meet George, who has been collecting for more than 70 years. Max finds a crate of vintage motor oil bottles that he thinks could be worth a small fortune

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In the picturesque Devon countryside, Henry Cole and Simon OBrien meet Derek and Daphne, who have decades of collecting stored in multiple barns. Henry finds a very rare tractor, which he has never found in a shed search before, while a Raleigh Shopper bike really lights Simons fire

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie welcome their guests to the Watersedge Guest House in Barton-on-Sea, Hampshire, but an afternoon of deer-watching leaves some of them bored. Desperate to impress their fellow BandB owners, the pair host a Hawaiian barbecue, before pulling out all the stops at breakfast time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owners Tim and Amelia Whitaker welcome their guests to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast, and take them out on a boat for a spot of seal watching and diving. However, the sight of wet suits proves too much for one contestant, and tensions also flare over dinner when Mike and Danny are quizzed about their relationship

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eva Buschmann hosts the competition at the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset. She makes the mistake of assuming Danny Robinson and Mike Gibson are a couple, and offers them a double room with an exposed shower, but hopes a lesson in abstract art and a meal at her favourite harbour-side restaurant will win the other contestants over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Gibson and Danny Robinson host their rival BandB owners at their four-star guesthouse - Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. The hosts take their visitors on a tram ride along the seafront and on to Madame Tussauds, but there are complaints about uncomfortable mattresses and disappointing views and the next days breakfast fails to impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and air their grievances. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie start the proceedings, but confront the guests who complained about their room having no en-suite facilities, before the winner is announced

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five cooks from in and around Harrogate, North Yorkshire, battle to win the £1,000 prize in the culinary contest. First to take on hosting duties is farm girl Jane Steele, who hopes a combination of innovative recipes and fancy wine will win over her guests - horse enthusiast Cassy Bell, photographer Simon Meyer, country gent Alasdair Moore and adventurous punk and cupcake seller Cathy McConaghy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Cassy Bell hosts the second dinner party in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, hoping her jockey-themed evening and gastro-pub menu will be enough to win her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Cathy McConaghy hosts the third dinner party in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where she decides to return to her Irish roots and serve a menu of home-made soda bread, cockle soup, oysters, roast lamb, and stout-infused sticky toffee pudding as she vies for the £1,000 prize. However, not all of her guests are impressed with the choice of food being dished up

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Simon Meyer is the fourth dinner-party host in Harrogate, North Yorkshire. He takes a laid-back approach to the task, but his chicken soup starter goes down so well the guests comments leave him struggling to retain his composure

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Country gent Alasdair Moore hosts the final dinner party in Harrogate, and hopes to impress his guests with his specially selected wines and proper menu. While his monkfish is well received, an early slip-up dents his confidence, and a risky parlour game does not endear him to farm lady Jane Steele. As the evening progresses, his competitors rate their experience, before the winner of the £1,000 cash prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Black Lion in Halland, East Sussex, where host Nigel Fright gets off to a shaky start when a kettle goes missing from one of the rooms. Later, he takes the guests to a salsa class, where Sherrill Tacy and Hayley Cotton are surprised by some of his dance moves

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to the Wheatsheaf Inn in Exning, near Newmarket, Suffolk, where guests are welcomed by owner Ken Clutterbuck and employee Ginny Trow. The proprietor tries to win his rivals round with his unique sense of humour, but tensions rise when the contestants go for a spin on a go-kart track

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sherrill Tacy hosts at the Sherwood Guesthouse in New Brighton on the Wirral, and is praised for the establishments value for money. However, there is upset when her breakfast leaves a lot to be desired, and her final review is far from perfect

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Final hosts Mark and Rachael Dann welcome the contestants to the Big Bear Lodge in Shropshire. They face the tough job of trying to keep the rift between some of the guests to a minimum in their ski-themed BandB, and their afternoon activity of canoeing raises a few laughs but accusations of cheating occur when the boys begin to get competitive

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants featured throughout the week reunite to revisit the highs and lows of their various stays, and as the BandB owners confront their critics face to face, they discover what the rival parties really thought of the quality of service that they encountered. After the deliberations are over, the winning guest house is announced

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help first-time buyers from Manchester. Robyn and Alice are struggling to decide between a starter flat and classic Corrie-style redbrick home. Steph and Christopher have been outbid 10 times even though theyve bid as much as £50,000 over asking price

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go head to head to help a couple who cannot decide to do with their home in Aylesbury, Buckinghamshire. Procurement manager Paul bought the house 13 years ago as a bachelor pad, but since cabin crew worker Sophie moved in they have had two children and he wants to move to a bigger, better family home. However, she grew up on the same street, her parents are just down the road and she loves having them on hand for babysitting. Can Kirstie make the necessary renovations to persuade Paul to stay? Or will Phil find a new good enough home to tempt Sophie away?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Marcus and Abi, who are building a luxurious off-grid family home deep in 20-acre woodland in Pembrokeshire on a budget of just £100,000. Granted permission through the One Planet Development planning laws, which are unique to Wales, they are armed with determination and timber from the surrounding forest. With no detailed plans, the project pushes the limits of design and endurance, and proves a raw, physical adventure that defies convention and challenges many of the ideas around what a home should be

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: A five-year-old is rushed into St Georges struggling to breathe, and her mum reveals how she has spent months in Great Ormond Street Hospital after being diagnosed with a rare form of juvenile arthritis. Meanwhile, an 83-year-old man in a confused state arrives in AandE after falling over, and as doctors carry out tests and a CT scan, his wife helps them understand the reason behind his confusion. Plus, a twelve-year-old girl must be brave as she undergoes treatment for a nasty cut on her leg

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Yorkshire, Neil needs urgent medical care after falling from his motorbike. The medics scramble to the Lake District when a walker goes into a cardiac arrest on the banks of Ullswater. Meanwhile, in Berkshire, Darran suffers nasty burns in an accident lighting a bonfire

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Cameras follow a 10-year-old who has severed his finger at school and a stonemason who has dropped a slab of marble on his hand. Meanwhile, a 74-year-old partially sighted woman is brought in by her neighbour after breaking her ankle in a fall, and draws the medical staff into stories of her challenging childhood

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A young mother suffers a panic attack while out with her three-year-old son. When paramedics arrive, shes gasping for breath. A two-year-old whos running a high temperature and has a history of respiratory problems needs to go to hospital - and fast. A deaf woman has collapsed in her bedroom and the crew needs to find out why shes fallen, though she cant understand what theyre saying. A 999 caller reports a man lying unconscious at a bus stop, but finding the patient proves difficult. Plus, paramedics are called to a man whos recovering from knee surgery

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: In search of the secret to the bubbles in bubble tea, Kate Quilton visits a cassava farm in Kenya to get to the root of it. Jimmy Doherty is in Somerset exploring why sour milk is sneered at, though soured cream is revered. Plus, Helen Lawal reports from Portugal on canned fish

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo head to Worcestershire where Max transforms old air tanks into a sports car, while Simon makes a trendy bar bench out of old barrels

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Cameras follow Lawrence, 26, who is rushed to St Georges after being hit by a car while crossing the road on a night out in Eastbourne. Meanwhile, 53-year-old Katrina is brought to AandE after having difficulty breathing and experiencing chest pains that could indicate a heart attack

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Nineteen-year-old motorcyclist Sam is airlifted to St Georges after crashing into a bus and being thrown under a car. He is placed in an induced coma and CT scans reveal Sam has bruising to his lungs, a ruptured kidney, a broken thigh bone and a fractured spine. The priority of the medics is is to stop his internal bleeding, while Sams parents are rushed to the hospital by police. Meanwhile, seven-year-old Raheem arrives in AandE with his dad Dennis after cutting his wrist while playing at home, and 59-year-old Therese turns up with a painful lump on her finger

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Surgeons must plan their next move carefully when Daisy, a 22-month-old girl, arrives at St Georges Hospital by air ambulance after being kicked in the head by a horse at her familys stables. Meanwhile, 54-year-old Mike, who has a form of medication-resistant epilepsy, arrives in minors with a bandage around his jaw after falling face-first onto the pavement. Plus, 100-year-old Florence, who was diagnosed with dementia three years earlier, and her daughter Frances arrive at the hospital when the former suffers what appears to be a stroke

programme.title: Supermarkets Unwrapped
programme.sub-title: Supermarkets Unwrapped: Fruit and Veg Aisle
programme.desc: The team take their trolleys down the fruit and veg aisle. Kate Quilton is in Kenya to see how summer vegetables get to British supermarkets in the thick of winter. Jimmy Doherty looks at the measures taken to transport bananas across the UK, John Whaite finds out why supermarket blackberries are so big, Helen Lawal investigates a robot berry-picker in Portugal, and Matt Tebbutt finds out how we can waste less fruit and veg

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien head to Wokingham in Berkshire to meet professional gardener Mark whose barns are brimful of bikes, bric-a-brac and baggage

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in Windsor, Berkshire, to meet Roy, whose garages are stacked full of bikes. Henry picks the one that doesnt work to take away, while Simon converts some 17th-century wooden beams into a pair of stylish bedside lamps

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of Guest and the City, Dan and Mardi Leonard, welcome their visitors in Brighton, but there is a mixed reaction to the couples contemporary decor and stained glass windows. The unconventional furniture leaves some guests confused, and one pair of fellow hoteliers bemoan the lack of sausages at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Phil Kiernan invites the contestants to the Farmers Boy Inn in Longhope, Gloucestershire, where he challenges them to a pie-making competition. However, the task proves less entertaining than the bickering that occurs between two guests as they try to share a kitchen

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Jane Asher host on the Geanna, their purpose-built hotel boat. Dan and Mardi Leonard are concerned space is too tight, while Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa worry about their privacy being invaded. A trip up the Thames allows the guests to sip champagne and get to know the ropes, but the mood at dinner is subdued, and when the owners cook their first breakfast on board they find themselves bickering

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Sarah Langford and Jean-Pierre Kujawa, who have run the Sentry Mead BandB on the Isle of Wight for six years, play host in the last challenge of the week. The guests arrive with high expectations and after a few setbacks, the dinner and breakfast win everyone over

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the weeks competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business consultant James Herbert hosts the first dinner party in Lichfield, Staffordshire, and has high hopes that his guests - football coach Tim Travers, sales assistant JP Goodyear-Martinez, businesswoman Amanda Smith and retired mental-health nurse Ronnie Imlah - will enjoy his complex Italian menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Retired mental-health nurse Ronnie Imlah hosts the second dinner party in Lichfield, Staffordshire, hoping her menu inspired by dishes she has eaten at friends events will win her the £1,000. A revelation by the host leaves her companions in shock, and two guests fail to see eye-to-eye on plastic surgery

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: JP Goodyear-Martinez hosts the third dinner party in Lichfield, Staffordshire, where he treats his guests to a mix of Jamaican food, tight Lycra outfits and a drag queen. However, James Herberts rendition of Queens Bohemian Rhapsody does not go down too well with fellow diner Ronnie

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Football coach Tim Travers hosts the fourth dinner party in Lichfield, Staffordshire, and is determined to prove his culinary skills, despite never having cooked before. He tackles an American-themed menu with the help of his mother, but discovers the hardest task is keeping the peace at the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Recruitment consultant Amanda Smith hosts the final dinner party in Lichfield, and hopes to impress with a well-executed, sophisticated menu. However the night is not to everyone elses liking and an accident at the table leaves her guests in hysterics, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Old Stables, Somerset, where Finnish-born Nina Dodd and her husband Andy welcome guests to their Scandinavian-style accommodation. While some are impressed by the immaculate interior, David and Erica Hensley are not pleased about the no-shoes policy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Rigsbys Guest House in Hertfordshire, where owner Morris Cockman entertains his rivals with a visit to a local zoo. Not all of his guests are impressed, and a slow breakfast service does not improve his chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Erica Hensley host at the Fairway in Blackpool, where they hope to win over their guests with a spot of classic seaside entertainment. After taking their fellow contestants on a horror walk, the couple will be wary of any unexpected surprises when it comes to their scores

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Robin Hughes is the final host of the week in the converted Dent Station in the Yorkshire Dales. He organises a butchery competition and high-end food, before some home truths about the group dynamic are revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. Robin Hughes remarks from across the week are discussed, before the winner of the competition is revealed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil help househunters in Edinburgh. Phil meets Freya and Elliot who are moving from Aberdeen to the capital for the cultural buzz. Kirstie helps Edinburgh woman Sadia and Irishman Sohail find their first family home so they can finally move out of Sadias starter flat

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Gary and Bill in the Edinburgh suburb of Portobello, who are unsure whether to sell their 19th-century four-bed home or renovate instead. Bill loves their homes character, quirkiness and close proximity to the seaside, but Gary hates the busy road and the homes layout, and would like to settle down somewhere new

programme.title: Bill Baileys Australian Adventure
programme.desc: The comedian makes a journey across Western Australia. In the first episode, Bill encounters true-blue Aussie locals, animals and landscapes in Western Australias far south. He visits the best beach in the world and the spectacular trees of the Valley of the Giants; goes paddleboarding in the chilly Southern Ocean with a former NBA star, and discovers the early stages of a secessionist movement

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on peoples expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.desc: Paul Murton tours Outer Orkney, beginning at North Ronaldsay, which may be the most northerly of the Orkneys but turns out to be surprisingly easy to get to. programme.title: Hitlers Gold
programme.desc: In Munich, in September 1919, history was about to be changed by a penniless army corporal thirsty to avenge his adoptive motherland. This episode examines the covert financial web that Hitler spun across Europe and around the globe to bankroll his dream of world domination

programme.desc: Using previously classified KGB documents, the second part of this film explores how far Soviet leaders were willing to go to cover up the Chernobyl nuclear disaster of 1986, including exposing children to toxic fallout at the Kyiv May Day parade to prove to the world that all was fine. Featuring first-hand accounts from those who survived the blast, including Oleksiy Ananenko, who braved radioactive waters to prevent a deadlier second explosion and Maryna Sivets, whose unborn childs life was put at risk

Featuring first-hand accounts from those who survived the blast, including Oleksiy Ananenko, who braved radioactive waters to prevent a deadlier second explosion and Maryna Sivets, whose unborn childs life was put at risk

programme.title: Hitlers Gold
programme.desc: In 1937, German leader Adolf Hitler appeared to be fulfilling his promise of returning his nation to prosperity. However, there was a deeply sinister side to the recovery. This episodes focuses on his efforts to bankroll his dream of world domination

programme.title: The Bush Years: Family, Duty, Power
programme.desc: How George HW Bush rose to vice president under Ronald Reagan. His son George W Bush committed to a life of sobriety and helped propel his father to the presidency

programme.title: Wrecks That Changed the World

This documentary features extensive interviews with historian Anna Reid, while eyewitnesses recall the dramatic events. Original diaries and documents, along with rarely seen film and photographic material, illustrate the tragedy

programme.desc: Paul Murton visits the islands of Coll and Tiree, which though just a few miles apart could not be more different. On Coll, he meets Nicolas Maclean Bristol, owner of Breachacha Castle, who can trace his ancestors on the island back to the 14th century. Tiree is one of the stormiest places in Europe, but is also one of the best places in Britain to surf and practise the relatively new sport of sand yachting, the racing of go-carts with a sail along the beach

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Mark Hill and Izzie Balmer - Day 3
programme.desc: The journey takes Mark Hill and Izzie Balmer through the Midlands. Starting in Royal Wootton Bassett and making their way to Cirencester, they collect treasures and trinkets in Stroud and Ashchurch. Mark becomes determined to uncover a bit of bling and finds the perfect piece in the form of a 1950s modernist pendant, plus a striking Victorian kettle designed by Dr Christopher Dresser. Izzie, meanwhile, takes a chance on some unusual Czechoslovakian glass, a Royal Worcester cup and a 19th-century food container. The pair then head off to sell their finds in Market Harborough, Leicestershire

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Mark Hill and Izzie Balmer - Day 4
programme.desc: Jewellery afficionado Izzie Balmer and 20th-century design and glassware connoisseur Mark Hill are back on the road in their classy 1987 Lotus Éclat, scouring Oxfordshire for treasures to take to auction. Mark sweeps past a 1930s model of Davy Crockett and splashes his cash instead on a Betjeman lockable decanter, a Doulton Lambeth inkwell and most of a Shelley drip ware coffee set. Izzie invests in a Minton majolica plate for serving strawberries, a set of French boutique display boxes and a tribal African babys hood

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with two searchers who both found family in a different country, including Roy David who was looking for his daughter, Cheryl. There is also an update on Maria Costantino. In 2018, she discovered her Italian birth mother had passed away, but met her large family

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit the case of Murray Phillips, who was looking for his older brother Ricky. There is also an update on Ann Melbourne, who traced her birth mother 14 years ago, and Jean Smith, who was looking for her American birth father, Johnny Perkins

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus and the team clear out an attic that is filled with Ferrari memorabilia and take the collection to auction to see how much it makes

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: The team helps a couple clear out their home in the seaside town of Filey, and Angus sees potential in some vintage toys and boxes of old soap

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Nick Hewer, host of Countdown and Alan Sugars former adviser on The Apprentice, tries to find out how his mothers Catholic father Oswald Jamieson managed to reach the prestigious office of High Sheriff in Protestant-dominated 1920s Belfast. On his fathers side, he researches the life of his nine times great-grandfather and discovers how his familys fortunes were shaped by the English Civil War. Narrated by Cherie Lunghi

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian Josh Widdicombe embarks on a roller-coaster ride through his extraordinary family history. After a less than promising start with an ancestor who was cut out of the family fortune, Josh is delighted to discover a courtier with very personal access to King Charles I. From there its back to Elizabethan and Tudor nobility and a royal love triangle

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate the case of Maria Roberts, the daughter of Italian parents who had a happy adoption growing up with an English family, and now hopes to find Gilda, her birth mother. The team also meets Francesca Barnes, who is in the early stages of transitioning from male to female. She was born with the name Paul and given up for adoption as a baby. Francesca is now searching for her mother, Norma, but fears her transition may prove too much of a challenge, and lead to rejection

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House

programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and the team clear out a huge aviation collection in Co Durham, then help a retired policeman sell items from his four-bedroom home

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Lesley Manville researches her family tree. So far as her mothers side is concerned, by delving deep into Brightons local archives, Lesley uncovers a complex story of wartime separation, adultery and the inaccessibility of divorce. Tracing back her fathers line sees Lesley trying to solve what happened to her ancestor Aaron Harding. A widower with a large family to feed, Aaron protested poor conditions and pay as an agricultural worker in 1830 - a protest that would become known as the Swing Riots. He was arrested and subsequently transported to Australia

David takes the technology route to DIY upskilling by using AI and YouTube tutorials. Healthcare worker Eleanor is getting first-hand training in lime plastering

programme.title: Red Eye
programme.desc: Tensions rise between Hana, Brody and Ambassador Tillman over whether the guests death was murder or a drunken accident. When Fox kills again, it proves Hana was right and that hes targeting the Ambassadors guests. Tennant is brought in to lead operations as Megan links both victims to testimony about a Russian sub downing the Samson D-300. Delaney presses Tillman and Peterson, who also testified, on why they blamed Russia. Shes troubled to learn an engineer who reported faults died suspiciously. Seeking answers, Hana goes to Tillmans office and ends up trapped in a lift with Fox-a ride she may not survive

programme.title: Any Given Day: Cork University Hospital
programme.desc: A patient having a heart attack is on his way to CUH from University Hospital Waterford, and in the paediatric allergy clinic, 11-year-old Ciaran is in for an oral food challenge. The emergency department team is standing by as a five-year-old girl is rushed to CUH. She is barely conscious and her vitals are poor. Surgeon James Harty is prepares for major surgery - the total replacement of a patients femur

programme.title: On the Beat
programme.desc: The massive challenges being faced by Gardaí across Ireland, concluding with a sensitive case in Monaghan in which a young woman is being extorted for money. Plus, the officers dealing with the growing shoplifting problem across the country

programme.title: Room to Improve
programme.desc: Architect Dermot Bannon and quantity surveyor Claire Irwin help empty nesters Deirdre and Kieran Kelly at Clonsilla in west Dublin. The couple are hoping to downsize from their spacious existing home to their beloved first home. To give Deirdre and Kieran the space they need, Dermot sets out to combine inside and outside with a triple-zoned ground floor, a glass corner extension and clever landscaping

As reporters flood Kilraven, Vivienne revels in the media frenzy while Emerson works to sabotage Patsys rivals in the election

To give Deirdre and Kieran the space they need, Dermot sets out to combine inside and outside with a triple-zoned ground floor, a glass corner extension and clever landscaping

programme.title: Here We Go
programme.desc: Comedy focusing on members of the Jessop family as they navigate a combination of lifes everyday challenges. Sue is downsizing and having a big clear-out, Amy is back from Norway and pining for the Fjords, and Rachels started her new university course. When Pauls old boat turns up in Sues garage, he decides to take the family out on the water, hoping it might cheer Amy up a bit. Meanwhile, Rachel is keen to convince her new classmates that, despite being literally twice their age, shes one of them. Shes planning to demonstrate this fact via the traditional art of the student drinking game. Alison Steadman, Katherine Parkinson, Jim Howick, Tom Basden, Freya Parks and Tori Allen-Martin star

For the showstopper, they tackle the decorative Swedish sandwich cake the smorgåstårta. Who will claim star baker - and who will be hanging up their apron?

programme.title: Scotlands Home of the Year
programme.desc: Architect Michael Angus and interior designers Anna Campbell-Jones and Banjo Beale take a look at the best houses in the country, beginning by focusing on the east. programme.title: Scotlands Home of the Year
programme.sub-title: North East and the Northern Isles
programme.desc: Judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Banjo Beale and Michael Angus visit three properties in the north-east and the Northern Isles. programme.desc: Richard tackles a shocking case on his first day back. Kavita and Jules prepare for a high-stakes presentation. Bailey has a difficult conversation with a beloved patient

programme.title: Bad Nanny
programme.desc: The first of a two-part documentary telling the story of Samantha Cookes - serial fraudster, master manipulator and, at one point, a trusted nanny. The film reveals one of her most brazen personas - Carrie Jade Williams, a supposedly terminally ill, award-winning author - which she used to scam her way across the UK and Ireland. She also invented the character of a nanny with a troubling secret, as well as several other audacious cons through the years

programme.title: Na Feilte Tine
programme.desc: Documentary exploring four indigenous Irish festivals of Samhain, Imbolg, Bealtaine and Lunasa. Each festival acts as a welcome to the new season. This edition takes a look at the festival of Samhain at the beginning of the Celtic new year, featuring a re-enactment of the ritual of the First Fire on the sacred hill of Tlachta

programme.title: Ambulance (2022)
programme.desc: Decorated veteran Will Sharp, desperate for money to cover his wifes medical bills, asks for help from the one person he knows he shouldnt - his adoptive brother Danny, a charismatic career criminal. Danny instead offers him a score - the biggest bank heist in Los Angeles history. With his wifes survival on the line, Will cant say no, but when their getaway goes spectacularly wrong, the desperate brothers hijack an ambulance with a wounded cop and an EMT onboard. Crime drama, directed by Michael Bay and starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Yahya Abdul-Mateen II and Eiza González

programme.title: Ireland in Music
programme.desc: Berlin-based singer Wallis Bird travels to Kells Courthouse in her native County Meath to share brand new songs and stories with presenter Denise Chaila

programme.title: Shakes Versus Shav (2023)
programme.desc: Short puppet movie based on the last play completed by George Bernard Shaw, in which the Irish playwright battles English bard William Shakespeare in a war of words to decide which of them is the better writer. Voiced by Colm Meaney and Derek Jacobi

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr fight against their maximalist taste to create a calming Balearic bedroom. The pair settle on an all-white, exposed natural brick feel. To nail their vision, the duo visit BandB rivals and local friends Ian and Andrews Ibiza-inspired holiday let, where bespoke storage and an innovative approach to the use of plaster get their creative juices flowing. Swapping out the boiler suits for Lycra, the pair later scope out Moclins famous cycling routes for future guests for the BandB and later still head to the Strait of Gibraltar for the trip of a lifetime

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr reach the last room of the Andalusian townhouse, deciding to go all-out designing a Moorish-themed bar. Amanda refuses to hold back and is ready and willing to bring in some opulence, keen to drench the room in patterned wallpaper and fabulous gold touches. Taking a break from the property, the pair head to a traditional tea house in the oldest neighbourhood in Granada - and get more than they bargained for when theyre treated to a surprise belly dance performance. Later, they reveal their completed labour of love with a big farewell party for their friends and family.

programme.desc: Maritime Inn landlady Pat gets her first full house of the season as Brixhams tourist trade picks up for the summer - but refuses to hire anyone to help her with the extra work. Powerboat instructor Mike Weatherbee tries to enforce the laws of the sea, carpet-cleaner Dave Satchwill prepares to lead the towns popular ghost tour, and lone fisherman Dave Brown reveals his unusual methods

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: August arrives and the fishermen of Brixham spend time ashore with their families as their boats are repaired - but summer proves costly for Jan, who loses £1,000 for every day his vessel is out of action.
