programme.desc: A nervous Alma and Ellsworth get married, and Tolliver informs George Hearst of Wolcotts murderous acts. When the engineer is confronted, he takes drastic action with devastating consequences. Wu launches an attack on Lee

programme.title: Lake George (2024)
programme.desc: Two misfits embark on a road trip, take a dubious deal and find redemption amid shady circumstances. Crime thriller, starring Carrie Coon and Shea Wingham

programme.title: Iron Monkey (1993)
programme.desc: In a desperate and unjust land, where government corruption rules the day, only one man has the courage to challenge the system and fight back. Thriller, starring Rongguang Yu and Donnie Yen. In Cantonese

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - April 2026
programme.desc: Previews of the films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights

programme.title: The Colour Room (2021)
programme.desc: Period drama telling the story of Clarice Cliff, who broke the glass ceiling and helped revolutionise the workplace in the 20th century. Clarice is a vivacious young factory worker in the industrial British midlands of the 1920s. Bursting with ideas for colours and shapes, she takes more and more dangerous risks, but manages to stay one step ahead of the workhouse and impress eccentric factory owner Colley Shorter with her talent and innovation. Starring Phoebe Dynevor, Matthew Goode and David Morrissey

programme.title: Chuck Chuck Baby (2024)
programme.desc: Helen lives with her ex-husband, his 20-year-old girlfriend, their new baby, and his dying mother Gwen. Helens life is a grind, but she has a chance at romance when a secret love returns. Writer/director Janis Pughs drama, starring Louise Brealey and Annabel Scholey

programme.title: Shazam! (2019)
programme.desc: Youngster Billy Batson is chosen by the ancient wizard Shazam as his new champion, and receives various superpowers. Together with his best friend Freddy Freeman, Billy must come to terms with his new talents in order to stop the evil Dr Thaddeus Sivana. Comedy adventure based on the Captain Marvel and Shazam comics, starring Zachary Levi, Mark Strong and Asher Angel

programme.title: Wonder Woman (2017)
programme.desc: An Amazon princess who grows up in enchanted realm has her first encounter with the outside world during the First World War. American airman Steve Trevor crash lands on her isolated island home. Determined to bring the chaos sweeping the world to an end, she ventures onto the front line in an attempt to bring down an ancient and powerful evil force that is driving the human race towards destruction. Director Patty Jenkins blockbusting fantasy adventure, starring Gal Gadot, Chris Pine and David Thewlis

programme.title: Arrival (2016)
programme.desc: The world is thrown into uproar when 12 alien spaceships arrive at locations around the globe. As tensions rise between nations and fear of the arrivals grows, a recently bereaved linguist is recruited by the US army to find a way to communicate with the aliens and discover why they have come. Sci-fi drama, starring Amy Adams, Jeremy Renner and Forest Whitaker

programme.title: Nightcrawler (2014)
programme.desc: A cynical petty crook is inspired when he sees a freelance camera crew, and decides to go into business filming crime scenes and accidents, then selling the footage to news broadcasters. His determination to stay ahead of the competition leads him down a dark path as his methods become increasingly ruthless. Crime drama, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Rene Russo and Bill Paxton

programme.title: Edge of Tomorrow (2014)
programme.desc: An inexperienced military officer is sent to the front line in a war against an alien invasion, and dies within minutes. However, at the point of death, he is transported back in time to relive his last day again. As he faces the same battle over and over again, the experience he gains turns him into the ultimate warrior. Sci-fi adventure based on Hiroshi Sakurazakas novel All You Need Is Kill, starring Tom Cruise, Emily Blunt and Brendan Gleeson

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga (2024)
programme.desc: In a post-apocalyptic world, a kidnapped girl grows into a warrior who seeks vengeance against a great biker horde led by the crazed Dementus. Director George Millers Mad Max: Fury Road prequel, starring Anya Taylor-Joy, Chris Hemsworth and Tom Burke

programme.title: Jericho Ridge (2023)
programme.desc: Trapped and alone, a small-town cop must fight for her life when her remote office is targeted by lethal enemies. Writer/director Will Gilbeys thriller, starring Nikki Amuka-Bird, Zack Morris and Chris Reilly

programme.title: The Colour Room (2021)
programme.desc: Period drama telling the story of Clarice Cliff, who broke the glass ceiling and helped revolutionise the workplace in the 20th century. Clarice is a vivacious young factory worker in the industrial British midlands of the 1920s. Bursting with ideas for colours and shapes, she takes more and more dangerous risks, but manages to stay one step ahead of the workhouse and impress eccentric factory owner Colley Shorter with her talent and innovation. Starring Phoebe Dynevor, Matthew Goode and David Morrissey

programme.title: Chuck Chuck Baby (2024)
programme.desc: Helen lives with her ex-husband, his 20-year-old girlfriend, their new baby, and his dying mother Gwen. Helens life is a grind, but she has a chance at romance when a secret love returns. Writer/director Janis Pughs drama, starring Louise Brealey and Annabel Scholey

programme.title: Funny Girl (1968)
programme.desc: Musical biopic of 1920s singer and comedienne Fanny Brice, who rose from poor beginnings in New Yorks slums to become an overnight star and the toast of Broadway. Her success, however, came at the price of a turbulent private life and an unhappy marriage to a gambler, who grew increasingly jealous of her fame. Starring an Oscar-winning Barbra Streisand, with Omar Sharif, Kay Medford and Walter Pidgeon

programme.title: The Death of Stalin (2017)
programme.desc: The place is Moscow, the year is 1953 and after being in power for nearly 30 years, Soviet dictator Joseph Stalin dies after an inconveniently brief illness. Amid the shock, the members of the Council of Ministers engage in a chaotic bid for power. Armando Iannuccis political satire, starring Andrea Riseborough, Jason Isaacs, Rupert Friend, Simon Russell Beale, Steve Buscemi, Jeffrey Tambor and Michael Palin

programme.title: Edge of Tomorrow (2014)
programme.desc: An inexperienced military officer is sent to the front line in a war against an alien invasion, and dies within minutes. However, at the point of death, he is transported back in time to relive his last day again. As he faces the same battle over and over again, the experience he gains turns him into the ultimate warrior. Sci-fi adventure based on Hiroshi Sakurazakas novel All You Need Is Kill, starring Tom Cruise, Emily Blunt and Brendan Gleeson

programme.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga (2024)
programme.desc: In a post-apocalyptic world, a kidnapped girl grows into a warrior who seeks vengeance against a great biker horde led by the crazed Dementus. Director George Millers Mad Max: Fury Road prequel, starring Anya Taylor-Joy, Chris Hemsworth and Tom Burke

programme.title: The Death of Stalin (2017)
programme.desc: The place is Moscow, the year is 1953 and after being in power for nearly 30 years, Soviet dictator Joseph Stalin dies after an inconveniently brief illness. Amid the shock, the members of the Council of Ministers engage in a chaotic bid for power. Armando Iannuccis political satire, starring Andrea Riseborough, Jason Isaacs, Rupert Friend, Simon Russell Beale, Steve Buscemi, Jeffrey Tambor and Michael Palin

programme.title: 48 Hrs (1982)
programme.desc: A hard-bitten cop goes on the trail of two escaped fugitives who killed several of his fellow officers. He needs someone with inside knowledge of the criminals, and is reluctantly forced to temporarily release a fast-talking thief from prison to help him in his investigation. Comedy thriller, starring Nick Nolte, Eddie Murphy and Annette OToole

programme.title: Tootsie (1982)
programme.desc: A New York actors difficult reputation has rendered him unemployable. When a TV soap advertises a new female role, he disguises himself as a woman for the audition - and wins the part. But the character proves so popular, he is forced to keep up the pretence indefinitely. Comedy, starring Dustin Hoffman, Jessica Lange and Bill Murray. Directed by Sydney Pollack, who makes a cameo appearance as Hoffmans agent

programme.title: Singin in the Rain (1952)
programme.desc: A silent-movie star tries to win the heart of an aspiring actress as the introduction of sound changes the film business for ever. However, his frequent on-screen love interest does not take kindly to this real-life romance. Musical comedy, starring Gene Kelly, Debbie Reynolds, Donald OConnor, Jean Hagen and Millard Mitchell. Songs include Make em Laugh, You Are My Lucky Star and the title number

programme.title: Risky Business (1983)
programme.desc: An upper-class suburban teenager gets involved with a prostitute while his parents are away on holiday, turning the family home into a brothel and jeopardising his plans for the future. Coming-of-age comedy, starring Tom Cruise, Rebecca De Mornay, Bronson Pinchot and Joe Pantoliano

programme.title: This Is Spinal Tap (1984)
programme.desc: An ageing British rock band try to revive their fortunes by embarking on a US tour, with a film-maker on hand to record the mayhem. However, the big comeback soon turns into a series of disasters as tensions between the clueless rockers and their long-suffering manager reach breaking point. Spoof documentary, starring Michael McKean, Christopher Guest, Harry Shearer and Rob Reiner

programme.title: The Brutalist (2025)
programme.desc: When a couple flee Europe in 1947, their new lives in the US are changed by a strange client. Director Brady Corbets drama, starring Adrien Brody, Felicity Jones, Guy Pearce and Joe Alwyn

programme.title: Thelma (2024)
programme.desc: After 93-year-old Thelma is conned by a phone scammer, she embarks on a risky journey across LA with only one thing on her mind - revenge. Comedy, starring June Squibb, Fred Hechinger, Parker Posey, Clark Gregg and in one of his final films, Richard Roundtree

programme.title: The Big Heat (1953)
programme.desc: A scrupulously honest detective investigates after a cop leaves a suicide note detailing corruption in the force and gangland connections, but gets taken off the case when his own wife is killed by the Mob. However, convinced the officers in charge are covering their tracks, he decides to go it alone. Fritz Langs noir crime drama, with Glenn Ford, Gloria Grahame, Lee Marvin and Alexander Scourby

programme.title: The Last Stop In Yuma County (2024)
programme.desc: While stuck at a rural Arizona rest stop, a salesman is thrust into a hostage situation by the arrival of two ruthless bank robbers. Writer/director Francis Galluppis crime thriller, starring Jim Cummings, Faizon Love and Jocelin Donahue

programme.title: Tootsie (1982)
programme.desc: A New York actors difficult reputation has rendered him unemployable. When a TV soap advertises a new female role, he disguises himself as a woman for the audition - and wins the part. But the character proves so popular, he is forced to keep up the pretence indefinitely. Comedy, starring Dustin Hoffman, Jessica Lange and Bill Murray. Directed by Sydney Pollack, who makes a cameo appearance as Hoffmans agent

programme.title: Roofman (2025)
programme.desc: Former US Army Reserve officer Jeffrey Manchester executes a plan to rob multiple branches of McDonalds. He is eventually captured by police and becomes the first person to escape from Brown Creek Correctional Institution in North Carolina. Jeffrey hides out at a toy shop for several months, and during forays outside the building, he sparks romance with store worker Leigh Wainscott. Comedy drama, starring Channing Tatum, Kirsten Dunst and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: One Battle After Another (2025)
programme.desc: Bob is a washed-up revolutionary who lives in a state of stoned paranoia, with his spirited and self-reliant daughter, Willa. When his evil nemesis resurfaces and Willa goes missing, he scrambles to find her as both battle the consequences of their pasts. Paul Thomas Andersons Oscar-winning action thriller loosely based on Thomas Pynchons novel Vineland, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, an Academy Award-winning Sean Penn, and Benicio del Toro

programme.title: Hostages (2025)
programme.desc: After an explosion at a London hotel, three people are swept up in the chaos. As rumours circulate that the blast was a terrorist attack, their story gathers momentum on social media and across the rolling 24-hour news cycle. Writer/director Jim Owens comedy thriller, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Tanya Moodie and Nicholas Asbury

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - April 2026
programme.desc: Previews of the films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights

programme.title: Regretting You (2025)
programme.desc: A mother and daughter grapple with whats left after a devastating accident reveals a shocking betrayal and forces them to rediscover themselves. Romantic comedy drama, starring Allison Williams, Mckenna Grace and Dave Franco

programme.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey (2025)
programme.desc: New York bachelor David is due to drive to his friends wedding, but a wheel clamp on his vehicle derails those plans. He acquires a new drive from the mysterious Car Rental Company managed by two nameless agents, who are oddly familiar with his dating history. At the wedding, David meets fellow singleton Sarah and squanders an opportunity to dance with her. On the way home, the rental cars GPS asks him if he would like to embark on a big bold beautiful journey. When David agrees, the virtual assistant guides him to Sarah and they follow a winding route between physical doors that open into episodes from their respective pasts. Romantic drama, starring Colin Farrell and Margot Robbie

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2 (2025)
programme.desc: Struggling to adjust to their newly minted lives as Good Guys, the Bad Guys are offered one last job by a group of all-female criminals. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron and Craig Robinson

programme.title: Weapons (2025)
programme.desc: At 2.17am, 17 children from the class of Justine Gandy run into the night, arms outstretched, never to be seen again. The only child from the class who remains safely in their bed is Alex Lilly. Distraught parent Archer Graff joins other members of the local community in pointing a finger of blame at Ms Gandy for the shocking turn of events. While she pleads innocence, school principal Andrew Marcus seeks answers along with police officer Paul Morgan. Horror, starring Julia Garner, Josh Brolin and Alden Ehrenreich

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: To take on the deepest wrecks in Scapa Flow, Ross needs to successfully navigate decompression training before heading out with local dive guide Emily Turton to dive on one of the biggest of the Kaiser?s Imperial war ships from the First World War, SMS Karlsruhe. As he explores the eerie relic, Ross tries to understand how and why the German fleet ended up on the Scottish seabed, when they had already surrendered months earlier. Having resolved one enduring mystery, he then heads 600 miles south to meet with ex-serviceman Mark Culwick and dive on an experimental British aircraft carrier submarine built in the 1920s called the M2. This unique vessel was lost in shocking circumstances, with all hands. During his dive Ross starts to understand the horrifying truth ofexactly how and why the 60 men on board never came home

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts follows archaeologists as they unearth Britains most spectacular iron-age grave, a chariot burial in East Yorkshire. programme.title: Commando Missions
programme.desc: Operation Frankton, of December 1942. The mission was for Royal Marines to place limpet mines on German ships in the occupied Bordeaux Harbour, 62 miles along the Gironde River. Five cockleshell canoes set off from submarine HMS Tuna at the mouth of the river. Only two reached the harbour and carried out the raid, escaping with the aid of the French Resistance. The damage to the ships and the resulting disruption to German supply lines had a significant impact on the war effort

programme.title: Commando Missions
programme.desc: Musketoon Operation, a daring, small-scale commando raid on a German-held electricity generating station at Glomfjord in Norway, carried out by British and Norwegian commandos in September 1942. The mission involved a submarine crossing, an overland route and the destruction of the power plant

programme.title: Commando Missions

programme.desc: The actor is joined by advanced technical diver Emily Turton in north Devon, as he prepares to dive down on the wreck of a sunken steam ship. Iona II was heading to America in the early 1860s, when it went down with a mystery cargo on board that the owners went to extensive efforts to recover in secret. The pair embark on a night dive with the hope it will reveal what the owners so desperately tried to covertly retrieve. Determined to get to the bottom of the mystery, Ross also meets with a maritime historian who has uncovered Iona IIs shocking purpose. programme.desc: The McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II. Regarded as one of the most important aircraft of the Cold War. It was a major part of the United States Air Force, Navy and Marine Corps, and was also used by many other countries

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts explore the disappearance of civilisations throughout time and across the globe, wondering if clues left in their wake point to an otherwordly explanation

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate whether alien life forms are thriving in Earths seas, following Russian cosmonauts discovery of living plankton in space

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Ultimate Evidence
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos decodes some of the most compelling evidence for Ancient Astronaut theory found in mysterious carvings, symbols and structures that are strikingly similar on opposite ends of the Earth. Could the answers to mankinds greatest questions about its origins and future actually be hidden in plain sight, disguised in the form of alien messages?

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Ultimate Evidence
programme.desc: An investigation into the Ark of the Covenant, one of the most sought after religious relics of all time and far more than just a box that contained the 10 Commandments. It apparently possessed divine powers that enabled it to produce food, take down stone walls, kill those who come in contact with it, and provide direct communication to God. If true, it may have had a link to an extraterrestrial past

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Ultimate Evidence
programme.sub-title: Aliens and the Creation of Man
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos takes an in-depth look at more extra-terrestrial mysteries, joining forces with theorists to consider whether unexplained advances in human evolution can be attributed to alien architects

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Ultimate Evidence
programme.desc: Ancient astronaut theorist Giorgio Tsoukalos investigates the mystery of the Nazca Lines, a series of ancient geoglyphs located in the desert area of southern Peru. Documentary exploring claims of supposed extraterrestrial visits to ancient civilizations

programme.title: Ancient Aliens: The Ultimate Evidence
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos questions whether Earths stone monoliths were built with extra terrestrial help. Documentary exploring theories regarding supposed extraterrestrial visits to ancient civilizations

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Aliens on the Sun, Megalodon in Japan, and the Extraterrestrial Cave
programme.desc: An examination of claims a retired Army aviation officer uncovered evidence of intelligent anomalies or technosignatures. Plus, Nasa-archived solar satellite photos and why a giant shark caught on camera sparked a debate about if megalodons still exist

programme.title: Hitlers Engineers
programme.desc: A profile of scientist Walter Heisenberg, who was key in the Nazis efforts to building an atomic bomb, asking how close he was to achieving his goal

programme.title: Historys Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: The McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II. Regarded as one of the most important aircraft of the Cold War. It was a major part of the United States Air Force, Navy and Marine Corps, and was also used by many other countries

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: An event in December 1917, when the SS Mont Blanc and the SS Imo accidentally crashed into each other in Halifax Harbour. The resulting explosion destroyed over 10,000 buildings and left approximately 25,000 people without shelter. Now, professional salvage diver Tony Sampson and his team to brave chilled waters in search of wreckage

programme.title: Rome: Rise and Fall of an Empire
programme.desc: How the Barbarian tribes brought about the Empires downfall, and the death of Emperor Orestes at the hands of mercenary soldiers

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts explore the disappearance of civilisations throughout time and across the globe, wondering if clues left in their wake point to an otherwordly explanation

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: An investigation into crop circles, which are a global phenomenon with 10,000 having been seen worldwide. The programme considers whether they are a hoax or evidence of alien interaction

programme.title: Alien Corridors
programme.desc: Recent records show California has the most UFO sightings of any US state. A team of multi-disciplined experts - believers and critics - investigates what these Alien Corridors are and what they mean. From Joshua Tree, where people have reported more sightings per capita, to Mount Shasta, whose residents experience the longest visitations, lasting an average of 51 minutes

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross is joined by maritime archaeologist Mallory Haas in Plymouth to deep dive on the wreck site of 36-gun Nelsonian frigate HMS Amethyst - a legendary battle cruiser, launched in 1799, which proved one of the most successful and prolific of the Napoleonic wars. Ross makes an incredible discovery using an underwater metal detector, and the historic item reveals why the ship was such a potent weapon of war, but also gives clues as to how and why it sank

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland, the graveyard of a Victorian workhouse sheds light on one of the most traumatic periods of modern Irish history, the Great Famine of 1845. A team from Sheffield University want to understand the lives of people who occupied a village near the famous caves at Cresswell Crags in Nottinghamshire, and near Lincoln, a revisit to an Anglo Saxon site near proves rewarding

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: Cameras capture the world from fresh perspectives. This episode features a long-forgotten network of Native American earthworks, a baffling battlefield mystery in Belgium, a mysterious blood red lake on a remote Greek island, and an eerie pink glow emulating from the middle of the Pacific Ocean

programme.title: Dirty Old Cars
programme.desc: In North Carolina a forgotten 53 Crestline goes from rat resort to regal ride. Then, in Minnesota, Jeremy and his team try to get decades worth of dust and dirt extracted from a special edition Mustang

programme.title: Dirty Old Cars
programme.desc: In Minnesota, a 1957 Dodge Coronet taken over by raccoons needs some care to get it looking like the Batmobile. In North Carolina, a neglected Honda Accord goes from dreadful to dazzling

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts investigate ancient mans apparent obsession with building pyramids, and consider whether extraterrestrials may have helped with their construction

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: An investigation into crop circles, which are a global phenomenon with 10,000 having been seen worldwide. The programme considers whether they are a hoax or evidence of alien interaction

programme.title: Hell on Earth: WWII
programme.desc: Documentary examining how the Japanese armies celebrated their victory following the attack on Pearl Harbour in December 1941. Four months later, however, their confidence was shaken by the Battle of the Coral Sea

programme.title: Hitlers Engineers
programme.desc: Hans Kammlers fingerprints can be found all over the Third Reichs most prestigious and deadly engineering projects, including rockets and concentration camps

programme.title: Hitlers Engineers
programme.desc: How the designer of one of the most popular car brands in history, Ferdinand Porsche, would not have seen his success without the support of Adolf Hitler

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. With the toughest landscape they had encountered in the nation, peopled by a rebellious tribe, it was no small task, but within a few years, the whole of Lakeland was under their control. Tony Robinson tackles the journey by himself, walking 50 miles from Penrith to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass, discovering that the Romans encountered beauty and danger in equal measure

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson takes a four-day trek along the coastline between Plymouth in Devon and Falmouth, learning about the thriving smuggling trade in Cornwall during the late 18th century. The areas tiny secretive harbours, beaches and secluded coves were ideal for illicit imports such as tobacco and brandy, and the business was so huge it threatened the national economy. Along his route, visiting such beauty spots as Lantic Bay and Polperro, he encounters all sorts of reminders of the trade and meets descendants of those involved

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Raksha Dave and Alex Langlands search for the remains of a First World War stable at Catterick Garrison in North Yorkshire, where thousands of horses would have been housed and trained for the front line. They join a team comprised of archaeologists and the military attempting to uncover the role this building played in the war effort. They also visit Britains oldest cavalry regiment, examining tools that soldiers would have used to look after their steeds, and hear the story of Warrior, known as the horse the Germans could not kill

programme.title: The Last Hunt for Nazi Gold
programme.desc: Chasing the myth in Poland as Guy Walters and Justine Ostrowska hunt the Nazi gold train, where obsession and absurdity blur legend and truth

programme.title: Secret Sex Lives of Tyrants
programme.desc: Uncovering the truth behind Chairman Mao, founder of the Peoples Republic of China, as a revolutionary image masks absolute power, luxury and ruthless control

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: When Rick reels in an antique glass fishing buoy, he hopes the sellers price floats his boat and Chum examines a rare dinosaur fossil

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In North Carolina, Mike and Jersey dive into a mad-scientist collection of antique quackery and medical equipment - all curated by Richard, a retired eye surgeon. Meanwhile, Larrys barn find features a multi-generational stockpile of vintage motorbikes, cars and leather gear

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Archaeologists conduct a dig at Auckland Castle in Co Durham, which 700 years ago was home to a group of power-crazed bishops who behaved more like warriors than religious leaders. The experts head to Dirleton Castle in East Lothian to reveal the role one of the men played in defending England from William Wallaces army, and also try their hands at the art of stained glass-making, and fire a working trebuchet - the ultimate medieval weapon of war

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal

programme.title: Digging Up Britains Past

They also visit Britains oldest cavalry regiment, examining tools that soldiers would have used to look after their steeds, and hear the story of Warrior, known as the horse the Germans could not kill

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Lisa hustles on a calm Long Beach day. Ivy and Pops make a vacation stop and outdo other buyers. Dave starts strong but gets playful. Brandi and Cameron nearly reach peak profits

programme.desc: The presenter and one-time Rolls Royce engineer fulfils a lifetimes ambition to visit the monuments of Ancient Egypt in a bid to understand how it could have been possible to produce such wonders of the ancient world 4,500 years ago. An expert guide is on hand to lay out the mainstream thinking and act as the perfect foil for Bradley and his theories

programme.sub-title: The Theft Of The Mona Lisa: Case Closed

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on another expedition through some of Britains most historic landscapes. He begins with a towpath exploration of industrial engineering along the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. Setting off from Liverpool, he takes a three-day walk inland to Wigan, ending at the towns iconic pier as he traces the story of the waterways chaotic construction and its impact on the development of Lancashire

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson explores the history of the ancient kingdom on a walk from Melrose in the Scottish Borders to Holy Island off the coast of Northumberland. Over five days, he goes on the trail of one of Northumbrias greatest sons - Saint Cuthbert. Through the Tweed Valley and across the Cheviot Hills, its a journey through a murky history laden with myth. However, by investigating the communities, power bases and landscape of seventh-century Northumbria, Tony aims to understand the pagan land in which Cuthbert spread his simple brand of Celtic Christianity

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Wolfe brothers Mike and Frank venture to South Carolina in search of hidden treasures. Their first stop is a remarkable grocery store frozen in time, offering a glimpse into the past. programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: The local economy of Palau relies heavily on eco-tourism. But as this documentary shows, the country is fighting a hard battle against man-made environmental destruction

programme.desc: Following the wild dog packs of Africa. Roaming with the pups of the Manzi and Kakumbi packs in South Luangwa, Zambia. Snowy faces a hyena before Weka must search for her offspring

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs Pride
programme.desc: Julz runs into a lion-hyena showdown and Henry goes on a house call to the Kakumbi den, where a pup has gone AWOL

programme.title: Over Australia
programme.desc: Two-party documentary featuring aerial footage of the Australian wilderness, beginning with the vast, dry plains of the interior, featuring the monolithic red rock of Uluru and the bounding red kangaroo

programme.title: Ferrari (2023)
programme.desc: During the summer of 1957, ex-racecar driver Enzo Ferrari is in crisis. Bankruptcy stalks the company he and his wife, Laura, built from nothing a decade earlier. Their tempestuous marriage struggles with the mourning for one son and the acknowledgement of another. Attempting to secure their survival, he rolls the dice on one race - 1,000 miles across Italy, the iconic Mille Miglia. Director Michael Manns drama, starring Adam Driver and Jack OConnell

programme.title: Amadeus (1984)
programme.desc: Composer Antonio Salieri is a favoured artist in the court of Austrian emperor Joseph II, but he finds himself pushed aside in favour of Mozart. Salieri admires the younger maestros music, but is incensed that someone so vulgar and obnoxious should be gifted with such talent. As jealousy consumes him, he plots his rivals downfall. Period drama, adapted by Peter Shaffer from his own play and starring F Murray Abraham and Tom Hulce

programme.title: Empire of the Sun (1987)
programme.desc: An English boy living in Shanghai is separated from his parents during the Japanese invasion and forced to live on the streets. The youngster is befriended by an American sailor, who helps the boy adapt to life without his family, before the pair are eventually captured and subjected to the terrors of internment. Steven Spielbergs Second World War drama based on JG Ballards semi-autobiographical novel, starring Christian Bale, John Malkovich and Nigel Havers

programme.title: Der Kaiser (2022)
programme.desc: Biopic focusing on the life of German soccer player and manager Franz Beckenbauer, starring Klaus Steinbacher and Teresa Rizos. In German

programme.title: Dance First (2023)
programme.desc: In 1969 Stockholm, Samuel Beckett is due to receive the Nobel Prize for Literature at a ceremony attended by his wife Suzanne and dignitaries. As he ascends the stage to accept the honour, Beckett sparks a conversation with himself about events and people that have led him to this point. Drama, starring Gabriel Byrne and Aidan Gillen

programme.title: Billionaire Boys Club (2018)
programme.desc: Led by their financial expert friend Joe Hunt, a group of young men in 1980s Los Angeles hatch a plan to get-rich-quick with a Ponzi scheme. However, the plan ends badly when Hunt and friend Jim Pittman decide to murder investor and con man Ron Levin. Co-writer/director James Coxs drama, starring Ansel Elgort, Taron Egerton and Emma Roberts.

programme.title: Captain Phillips (2013)
programme.desc: An American cargo ship sets a dangerous course around the coast of Somalia, while inland, four men are pressed into service as pirates by the local warlords. The captain is taken hostage when the raiding party hijacks the vessel, resulting in a tense five-day crisis. Fact-based thriller, starring Tom Hanks and Barkhad Abdi

programme.title: The Post (2017)
programme.desc: In 1971, an American military analyst uncovers the depths of the US governments deceptions about the futility of the Vietnam War, and takes action by copying top-secret documents that would become the Pentagon Papers. Washington Post owner Kay Graham and her editor Ben Bradlee discover The New York Times is preparing an explosive expose on those papers and, determined to compete, set out to publish them first. Steven Spielbergs fact-based drama, starring Meryl Streep, Tom Hanks, Sarah Paulson and Bob Odenkirk

programme.title: Dunkirk (2017)
programme.desc: Allied soldiers from Belgium, the British Empire and France are surrounded by the German army, and face a tense wait for evacuation, during a fierce battle in northern France during the Second World War. Christopher Nolans wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.title: Saturday Night (2024)
programme.desc: During the build-up to the first broadcast of Saturday Night Live on October 11th 1975, a group of young comedians and writers prepare to change television forever. Comedy, starring Gabriel LaBelle, Rachel Sennott, Cory Michael Smith and Ella Hunt

programme.title: The Glorias (2020)
programme.desc: The story of feminist icon Gloria Steinems itinerant childhood and its influence on her life as a writer, activist and organiser for womens rights worldwide. Director Julie Taymors biopic, starring Julianne Moore, Alicia Vikander, Ryan Kiera Armstrong and Lulu Wilson, who all portray Steinem at different points in her life. With Janelle Monae and Bette Midler

programme.title: Funny Girl (1968)
programme.desc: Musical biopic of 1920s singer and comedienne Fanny Brice, who rose from poor beginnings in New Yorks slums to become an overnight star and the toast of Broadway. Her success, however, came at the price of a turbulent private life and an unhappy marriage to a gambler, who grew increasingly jealous of her fame. Starring an Oscar-winning Barbra Streisand, with Omar Sharif, Kay Medford and Walter Pidgeon

Christopher Nolans wartime drama, which he wrote, directed and produced, starring Kenneth Branagh, Tom Hardy, Mark Rylance and Fionn Whitehead

programme.title: Rob Peace (2024)
programme.desc: A young man is torn between his fathers shadowed past and his own promising future. Raised by his devoted mother, Rob Peace risks everything he has worked for to free his imprisoned dad. Drama, starring Chiwetel Ejiofor, who also adapted Jeff Hobbs book and directed, with Jay Will, Camila Cabello and Mary J Blige

programme.title: Widow Clicquot (2023)
programme.desc: In the late 18th century, a group of women create the Veuve Clicquot champagne business. Drama, starring Haley Bennett, Tom Sturridge and Sam Riley

programme.title: The Survivor
programme.desc: Drama based on the story of Harry Haft, a survivor of the Auschwitz concentration camp who boxed his fellow inmates to stay alive. Haunted by memories and guilt, he attempts to use high-profile fights against boxing legends like Rocky Marciano as a way to find his first love again. Directed by Barry Levinson and starring Ben Foster, John Leguizamo, Peter Sarsgaard and Danny DeVito

programme.title: Mollys Game (2017)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about Molly Bloom, an Olympic-class skier who ran the worlds most exclusive high-stakes poker game for a decade before being arrested in the middle of the night by FBI agents wielding automatic weapons. Her players included Hollywood royalty, sports stars, business titans and finally - unbeknownst to her - the Russian Mob. Directed by The West Wing creator Aaron Sorkin, and starring Jessica Chastain, Idris Elba and Kevin Costner

programme.title: Jackie (2016)
programme.desc: Following the assassination of her husband, President John F Kennedy, Jacqueline Kennedy battles with grief and trauma to regain her faith, console her children, and define JFKs legacy. Director Pablo Larrains Bafta-winning drama, starring Natalie Portman, Peter Sarsgaard and Greta Gerwig. Mica Levi provided the Oscar-nominated score

programme.desc: In the late 18th century, a group of women create the Veuve Clicquot champagne business. Drama, starring Haley Bennett, Tom Sturridge and Sam Riley

Public anger reaches a peak after the incident, as relationships sour with authority. Fact-based drama set during the 1967 Detroit riots, starring John Boyega, Anthony Mackie and Algee Smith

programme.desc: Sigmund Freud invites CS Lewis to debate the existence of God. Drama, starring Anthony Hopkins, Matthew Goode and Liv Lisa Fries

programme.title: Warehouse 13
programme.desc: Pete and Myka get sucked into a 1940s detective noir novel in search of an artefact, while Artie, Steve and Claudia chase down a phantom car thief

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: A decade into the future, Carter and Dr Fraiser uncover the shocking truth behind an alien race that gave Earth a cure for cancer, and face a race against time to safeguard the future of humanity. Sci-fi drama, starring Amanda Tapping, Teryl Rothery and Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: The Doctor simulates a holographic family in a bid to understand relationships - but BElanna interferes with the program. Sci-fi drama, starring Robert Picardo and Roxann Biggs-Dawson

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Picard and Data discover the crew of the Enterprise has turned into prehistoric beings. Sci-fi drama, starring Patrick Stewart and Brent Spiner

programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine

programme.title: Stargate SG-1

programme.title: From
programme.desc: Jim, Tabitha and Ethan begin asking questions about where they are in the hopes it may lead them home, while Ellis and Fatima show Julie the lighter side of life in town. Jade struggles to make sense of their situation, while Boyd seeks guidance

programme.title: From
programme.desc: Boyds plan to head off into the forest provokes an unexpected response from Kenny, while Jade enlists Jims help with an experiment. Tabitha makes a chilling discovery that sends her down a unique path searching for answers.

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Rosalee stands on the jury in the trial of a lion-like Wesen accused of killing his wife. However, it becomes apparent the defendants lawyer possesses magical powers which he is using to bend the jurors to his will, and Rosalee falls under the spell. Fantasy drama, starring Bree Turner

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: A fire monster kills a geologist gathering rock samples from a volcano, while Juliette struggles to cope with her lost memories of Nick, repeatedly seeing visions of him. In Europe, a pregnant Adalind meets a powerful new ally. Fantasy drama, starring David Giuntoli and Bitsie Tulloch

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: Rick and the others collaborate with the new group of survivors to clear another prison wing of zombies, but it is an uneasy alliance. Carol carries out a Caesarean section on a dead walker as practice for the operation she may have to perform on Lori. But an unseen pair of eyes is keeping watch on her progress. Post-apocalyptic drama, starring Andrew Lincoln

programme.title: From
programme.desc: The Matthews family must choose which of the two settlements to join, while Jade comes to terms with his situation and Boyd is faced with an impossible decision

programme.title: The Swarm
programme.desc: Venice sees an unprecedented plague of jellyfish while in Canada researcher Anawak is getting to the bottom of whale attacks

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Part one of two. Yvette Fielding begin a 2007 investigation at the Transylvanian home of the notorious Vlad The Impaler Tepes, the man on whom the legend of Dracula was based. Originally broadcast live from Corvins Castle, the boyhood home of Vlad Tepes, the first night of the ghost-hunt sees the team searching the Transylvanian Room, Desert Tower and the Hanging Gallery

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Part two of two. Yvette Fielding and the team continue their 2007 Transylvanian investigation at the home of the notorious Vlad The Impaler Tepes, the man on whom the legend of Dracula was based. The second night in Corvins Castle sees them search the Guards Passageway, the Old Gate Tower, the White Tower and the Bethlen Wing.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotlands Lochs
programme.desc: Landing at Glenuig, Paul Murton makes his way inland to Loch Moidart and MacDonald country, walking the old coffin road, which was used for centuries by funeral parties, taking their dead to a sacred isle. On the track Paul meets a trials bike enthusiast and learns the secrets of a dabbing coat before landing off the Green Isle, where he rings a 1,100-year-old bell for the dead. Taking a boat north, Paul sails up Loch Shiel to Glenfinnan where Bonnie Prince Charlie officially began his rising to reclaim the throne for the Stuart Dynasty. From a forgotten monument that commemorates this event, Paul heads back to the coast and Loch Ailort, where he learns how to knife fight before taking a trip across the loch to a deserted community on the roadless Ardnish peninsula

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull are challenged to turn a tired old garden into a space inspired by the Middle East. John and Vanessa want a garden reminiscent of Oman, the desert kingdom where they first met. programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Becky and Paul and their two children live on the edge of the picturesque Quantocks near Taunton, Somerset, but are struggling with the strange dog-legged shape of their new-build garden. Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull use a £6,000 budget to transform its landscaping and introduce a cracked paving patio and bespoke seating area, with a dry stone wall façade. While Chris drives the main garden build, Charlie get to tackle the dog-leg, which Chris has designed to be a fun kids area with a water play area and wooden musical instruments

programme.title: Love Your Garden

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Rick ends his UK food tour on home turf, the West Country, where he meets a trio of food pioneers - Riverfords Guy Singh-Watson, who blazed a trail for organic food before many people knew what it was, famous cheese-maker Mary Quicke and his friend and fellow chef Mark Hix, the master of simple British food. Back home Rick cooks a carrot tarte tatin and a cheese, potato and spinach filo pie

programme.title: Come Play With Me (1977)
programme.desc: A nurse at a health farm takes over running the establishment, and comes up with her own range of sexual treatments. Meanwhile, two forgers on the run hide out at the farm. Sex comedy, starring Mary Millington, Alfie Bass and Irene Handl

programme.title: Country House Auction
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Chris gets his hands on an inscribed Egyptian amulet and gives trainee auctioneer James a masterclass in ancient signs and symbols. A historical silver pin cushion, which was once owned by a leading lady of the Irish Arts and Crafts movement, piques the interest of competing buyers. Plus, auctioneer James gets up close and personal with the inner workings of some valuable watches

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Mark Evans meets more people who aspire to live in their ultimate waterside dream home. Simon and Mary Sparrow live on a barge in Beccles, Suffolk, with their son Nate and their two cats. The Sparrows have been running a boatyard for nearly 10 years, specialising in refurbishing and renting out floating homes to holidaymakers. Now theyre designing and building a new, one-of-a-kind floating home for themselves - a futuristic-looking floating pod, drawing on a budget of £70,000

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and the team are called upon to investigate the death of Fidels old school friend, Carlton Paris, who has been shot at home. The victim was last seen alive at an art gallery with Dorothy Foster, a wealthy older woman - and before long they discover he made his living as an escort and had left several disgruntled and heartbroken women in his wake, including a local judge and the wife of a club owner. But as the investigation progresses, Fidel struggles to keep his emotions in check, forcing him to confront some long-buried issues. Kris Marshall and Gary Carr star, with guest appearances from Adrian Scarborough, Sharon Small, Tristan Gemmill and Josette Simon

programme.title: Miss Scarlet and the Duke
programme.desc: Dylan Cooper has been nabbed, but his gang plan to spring him out of the cells at Scotland Yard, leading to a long night for Blake and new detective George Willows

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A flight attendant is poisoned during her crews overnight stay on the island. Humphrey and his team are called to the victims hotel and begin by questioning her colleagues, but with so many suspects and so little to go on, they are left scratching their heads trying to work out which of her colleagues wanted her dead - so Dwayne goes rogue to pursue his own line of inquiry. Kris Marshall and Danny John-Jules star in the light-hearted Caribbean crime drama

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: The murder of a trainer at a baseball academy leads Cho, Lisbon and Jane to suspect the crime could have been motivated by money. programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Ogden, Watts and Effie attend a health retreat run by a flamboyant and charismatic leader where a fellow guest dies mysteriously

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: When a champion racehorse is found dead in his stall at the Rosedale Stables, Murdoch and Brackenreid investigate the other horse owners and trainers - including Violet Hart.

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: The murder of a trainer at a baseball academy leads Cho, Lisbon and Jane to suspect the crime could have been motivated by money. The team learns his wife is sole beneficiary of a substantial life insurance payout, and his business partner stood to take the controlling share of their company - but a lack of evidence hampers the investigation - until the consultant follows a hunch. Jane also has a flashback when he is knocked out, leading him to remember working at a circus with his father. Guest starring Jude Ciccolella

programme.title: Hudson and Rex
programme.desc: Charlie and Rex are in deep water after a university rower is shot by a sniper during team practice

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: Lucy Lawless stars as Alexa Crowe, a cop-turned-private investigator called upon to provide professional support and advice to ex-colleague and long-time friend DI Kieran Hussey. A wealthy socialite dies in suspicious circumstances, leaving her entire fortune to her cat, and Alexa must investigate a heady world of high society gossip to uncover the truth

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Tensions rise to deadly levels when a performance troupe of anarchists arrive in Kembleford, leaving Mrs McCarthy unimpressed and Bunty inspired

programme.title: Death in Paradise

programme.title: Saint-Pierre
programme.desc: Crime drama following a Newfoundland cop (Allan Hawco) exiled to Saint-Pierre et Miquelon, where picturesque isolation masks dangerous secrets. Also starring Death in Paradises Josephine Jobert. New-to-town Inspector Donny Fitz Fitzpatrick joins forces with local detective Genevieve Archambault (Josephine Jobert) to solve the murder of a back-to-nature group leader

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A visit to Chummys holiday camp leads to another murder mystery for the sleuthing cleric. Mark Williams stars, with a guest appearance by Ted Robbins

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Tensions rise to deadly levels when a performance troupe of anarchists arrive in Kembleford, leaving Mrs McCarthy unimpressed and Bunty inspired

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Richard Poole meets up with a group of old university friends who have arrived on the island for a reunion, only for tragedy to strike when the man who has solved so many murders becomes the victim. Faced with such a sensitive crime, the commissioner quickly drafts in another British inspector to help the team get to the bottom of the mystery. It soon becomes clear that the newcomer, Humphrey Goodman, is just as eccentric as Poole - but he is also just as bright, and wastes no time questioning the partygoers, all of whom are prime suspects, while pondering the biggest mystery of all - how did the culprit commit the crime in full view of all the other guests? Kris Marshall joins the cast, with Ben Miller, Sara Martins and Danny John-Jules

programme.title: Worlds Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Twenty Twelve co-stars Hugh Bonneville and Jessica Hynes pair up again to go on a road trip through the former Soviet republic of Georgia. Driving from the border with Azerbaijan to the Black Sea, Jessica soon discovers her spirit of adventure is causing Hugh a few moments of high anxiety, but there is worse to come when they find themselves stuck on a high mountain pass in bad weather and he has to overcome his vertigo. While the drive is a challenge all the way, the country and its people delight and surprise them at every turn

programme.desc: Footballer Raheem Sterling is one of a string of multi-millionaires targeted by a highly-organised burglary gang being pursued by Surrey police

programme.desc: Scotty transports a $30,000 haystack but has a problem with his trailer, a town that Jake is heading to is on fire, and Des tries to make it up a very steep hill

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the post-war British modern art movement in the seaside town of St Ives, the home for leading artists such as Barbara Hepworth and Terry Frost. At the Lizard Peninsula, Rick heads out to sea to fish for red mullet, and on the wild coast at Lands End, he discovers why Cornwall has a rather unusual animal as its national emblem

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwalls most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the countys oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: An RSPCA Special Ops team investigate an international criminal puppy farming operation, and Lancashire officers prosecute an owner for animal cruelty

programme.desc: The Serious Fraud Office go undercover to investigate a suspect holiday home company offering exclusive residences in the Caribbean

programme.title: The Boat That Guy Built
programme.desc: Guy Martin visits an Industrial Revolution cotton mill, where he weaves his own bedsheets on 19th-century looms. He then makes a mattress at the same factory that supplies the Queen, and creates his own version of an alarm clock

programme.title: The Boat That Guy Built
programme.desc: Motorcycle racer and engineering enthusiast Guy Martin sets out to enjoy beans on toast. He begins by making a tin can using a 200-year-old technique, then bakes his own bread, and learns about Sheffield-steel cutlery

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate the traditional British picnic, meeting mothers who consider themselves experts at creating home-cooked delicacies for enjoying in the great outdoors. programme.title: Worlds Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Comedians Phill Jupitus and Marcus Brigstocke head to Bolivia for a high-altitude trip, first driving along the infamous Road of Death, only to find themselves deep in bandit country. Disorientation takes them by surprise as they have to navigate Salar de Uyuni - a salt lake so vast that Buzz Aldrin could see it from space. Their goal is the ancient mountain city of Potosi, whose silver mines financed the Spanish empire in the 16th century and which is almost 5,000 metres above sea level

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: City of London Police begin a dangerous high-speed chase when the UKs most notorious phone snatcher races into the Square Mile on an illegal electric bike

programme.title: Highland Cops
programme.desc: The work of Police Scotlands Highlands and Islands division. A bushcraft expert is reported missing in remote Sutherland and officers battle to find which route he took in order to locate him before a blizzard sets in. Narrated by James Cosmo

programme.title: Aussie Road Train Truckers
programme.desc: Scotty and Shaun travel to one of the most remote cattle stations in the world, Glenn and Dave attempt a cement operation, and Brownie races to beat the rain

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwalls most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britains greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penwith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwalls oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: Counter Terrorism Policing foil an Islamist terror plot, and the CPS Special Crime Unit bring those responsible for a fatal crash on the M62 to justice

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: Manchester police take down a gang of teen car thieves, and the Serious Fraud Office unravels a 400-million-pound global fraud in the steel industry

programme.title: The Boat That Guy Built
programme.desc: Guy Martin enlists the help of a friend as he gives his narrowboat a makeover. They learn about designer William Morris while applying one of his wallpaper patterns, and try their hand at painting and traditional decorative treatments. Guy then heads to a Macclesfield mill where he is taught how to weave silk for interior decorations

programme.title: The Boat That Guy Built
programme.desc: To celebrate the end of the project, Guy Martin shows off the renovated boat at a party, learns how to prepare Victoria sponge, and shoots a commemorative Victorian-style portrait with a 150-year-old camera. However, the maiden voyage is hampered by bad water and weather conditions, as the narrowboat struggles through inch-thick ice

programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate the traditional British picnic, meeting mothers who consider themselves experts at creating home-cooked delicacies for enjoying in the great outdoors. The pair reveal their own take on the Scotch egg, and demonstrate a complex Victoria sponge recipe. Plus, a Birmingham mum prepares a selection of spicy Indian treats

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the post-war British modern art movement in the seaside town of St Ives, the home for leading artists such as Barbara Hepworth and Terry Frost. At the Lizard Peninsula, Rick heads out to sea to fish for red mullet, and on the wild coast at Lands End, he discovers why Cornwall has a rather unusual animal as its national emblem

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the Roseland Peninsula, beginning at a spot believed by some to have been visited by Jesus Christ. He tours the bay of St Mawes with Alex and Olga Polizzi, who own one of Cornwalls most luxurious hotels, and meets one of the countys oldest butchers, who demonstrates the ancient practice of aging meat. Finally, in the harbourside village of Mousehole, Rick meets a potter who finds inspiration from the local landscape

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy actress Katherine Parkinson, dance guru Louie Spence, comedian David ODoherty and breakfast news presenter Bill Turnbull join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon. The aim of the game is to hoodwink opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies - like whether Louie makes himself cry before every performance and if David actually is seeing a psychiatrist to cure a strange addiction

programme.title: Worlds Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Comedians Phill Jupitus and Marcus Brigstocke head to Bolivia for a high-altitude trip, first driving along the infamous Road of Death, only to find themselves deep in bandit country. Disorientation takes them by surprise as they have to navigate Salar de Uyuni - a salt lake so vast that Buzz Aldrin could see it from space. Their goal is the ancient mountain city of Potosi, whose silver mines financed the Spanish empire in the 16th century and which is almost 5,000 metres above sea level

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick visits Tintagel, Cornwalls most mythical place, which was an important trading port with Europe and the birthplace of perhaps Britains greatest legend - the tale of King Arthur. He also learns how the Methodists were important to Cornwall, and how they would cook a rather unusual bun during feast days, and meets a family on the Roseland Peninsula who are continuing the surprisingly ancient tradition of growing an exotic spice - saffron

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick explores the rich and diverse history of West Penwith, the farthest point west in mainland Britain. He visits Lamorna Cove, made famous through song and art, and the Unesco-protected tin and copper mining area of Botallack, where he talks to a mining explorer. The chef also follows an ancient track in search of Cornwalls oldest building and reveals how, as a young lad on the Lizard Peninsula, he fell in love for the first time and tasted his first French fish stew - known as a bouillabaisse

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal

programme.title: The Boat That Guy Built

The quest for the truth finds Dalziel and Pascoe uncovering dark family secrets, including murder, betrayal, mad dogs, gay bars, young love and a revealing birthmark. programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of December 3, 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. Ten days later, Agatha was discovered in a hotel in Harrogate, claiming to have lost her memory. Lucy Worsley delves into this traumatic, watershed moment in Christies life. She visits the key locations, from the chalk pit where Agatha crashed her car to Abney Hall, the grand house where she took refuge (and which would inspire several of her books)

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: In 1930, recovering from a personal crisis, Agatha Christie fell in love with and married Max Mallowan. This relationship, and the security it brought, saw Agatha enter the most prolific and successful chapter of her career. This would be the period in which Agatha penned some of her most famous and enduring bestsellers, including Murder on the Orient Express. Lucy follows in Agathas footsteps to discover the roots of these works and explores how, in later life, Agatha achieved global celebrity - but remained very much the anonymous observer, hiding in plain sight

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: An examination of the dark history behind Britains fascination with murder, beginning with historian and curator Lucy Worsley exploring how notorious killings were transformed into popular entertainment in the first half of the 19th century

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevensons Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public

programme.sub-title: Death to the Daleks - Part Four
programme.desc: British sci-fi adventure starring Jon Pertwee. The Doctor and Bellal penetrate the citys defences while Sarah and Jill try to stop the Daleks from escaping with the parrinium

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: On the evening of December 3, 1926, Agatha Christie left her home in Sunningdale, Berkshire, in her Morris Cowley. She visits the key locations, from the chalk pit where Agatha crashed her car to Abney Hall, the grand house where she took refuge (and which would inspire several of her books)

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The latest stage of Michael Portillos trek takes him past lochs and castles on the Far North Line to Britains northernmost railway station at Thurso. From Lairg on the banks of Loch Shin, Michael heads for a secret conservation area in the 18-mile-long loch to track down one of Scotlands most elusive and endangered species, the freshwater pearl mussel

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Martin Clunes: Islands of America
programme.desc: The actor arrives in New York to explore Manhattan Island, and its Empire State Building, from where he can see the citys 500 miles of coastline and more than 40 islands. He later takes the ferry to Ellis Island, which opened in 1892 as an immigration centre for those arriving in the US by sea. Following a trip to Coney Island, home to the iconic amusement park, he explores Marthas Vineyard more than 200 miles away, where Jaws was made. Martin ends his trip in Maine, Americas most easterly state, with hundreds of islands off its coast

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the worlds most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates what rising temperatures will mean for people and wildlife in the polar regions, and how changes there could affect the rest of the planet. He discovers polar bears in the Arctic are going hungry as the sea ice they hunt on disappears, and accompanies scientists studying waterfalls of meltwater in Greenland that are leading to a rise in global sea levels. From the Antarctic there are images of the break-up of the Wilkins Ice Shelf, which covered an area the size of Jamaica

programme.desc: Classic British sci-fi adventure starring Jon Pertwee. The Doctor forms an unlikely alliance with the Daleks to mine the parrinium only to come under attack from the Exxilons

programme.sub-title: Death to the Daleks - Part Three

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The documentary examines the actions of Hitler and his inner circle as the Third Reich faced collapse in the final weeks of the Second World War. Germany was losing the war but Hitler refused to surrender, instead pursuing a policy of Total War, which played out as a psychological drama within his inner circle

Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough investigates what rising temperatures will mean for people and wildlife in the polar regions, and how changes there could affect the rest of the planet. He discovers polar bears in the Arctic are going hungry as the sea ice they hunt on disappears, and accompanies scientists studying waterfalls of meltwater in Greenland that are leading to a rise in global sea levels. From the Antarctic there are images of the break-up of the Wilkins Ice Shelf, which covered an area the size of Jamaica

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: Death to the Daleks - Part One
programme.desc: The TARDIS is diverted to the barren planet Exxilon where the Doctor discovers the Daleks are searching for a cure to a space plague

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: Death to the Daleks - Part Two
programme.desc: Classic British sci-fi adventure starring Jon Pertwee. The Doctor forms an unlikely alliance with the Daleks to mine the parrinium only to come under attack from the Exxilons

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: Death to the Daleks - Part Three
programme.desc: Classic British sci-fi adventure starring Jon Pertwee. The Doctor and Sarah meet with Bellal, who helps them evade the Daleks and explains the origin of the city

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.sub-title: Death to the Daleks - Part Four
programme.desc: British sci-fi adventure starring Jon Pertwee. The Doctor and Bellal penetrate the citys defences while Sarah and Jill try to stop the Daleks from escaping with the parrinium

programme.sub-title: Genesis of the Daleks - Part One
programme.desc: Classic sci-fi adventure starring Tom Baker. The Doctor and his companions are dispatched by the Time Lords to the planet Skaro to prevent the creation of the nefarious Daleks

programme.sub-title: Genesis of the Daleks - Part Two

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines Britains deadliest pandemic, exploring how its monumental death toll changed the face of the country for ever and what the sudden loss of life meant for the church and religious beliefs, landowners and for the those who survived. Lucy also learns how DNA extracted from the skeletons of the plagues victims enabled scientists to finally identify a bacteria called Yersinia Pestis - a pathogen to which the mediaeval population had no immunity

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The documentary examines the actions of Hitler and his inner circle as the Third Reich faced collapse in the final weeks of the Second World War. Germany was losing the war but Hitler refused to surrender, instead pursuing a policy of Total War, which played out as a psychological drama within his inner circle

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: At the end of March 1945, Nazi Germany is on the brink of collapse and with Berlin under daily bombardment, Hitler permanently retreated to his bunker, a series of 30 cramped rooms under the Reich Chancellery, where night and day merge into one. As his life becomes increasingly bizarre, Hitler and Joseph Goebbels look for signs from German folklore that fate will intervene

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville changes his image with the help of some trendy clothes and a medallion made from a cocoa tin lid, while Arkwright decides to increase his profits through advertising

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony develops a sudden interest in astrology when Deborah announces her fascination with the mystical art. Meanwhile, Gary disapproves of the new pub landlords policies about lock-ins and bar tabs. Neil Morrissey, Leslie Ash and Martin Clunes star

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Guy showers Lucy with expensive gifts before taking her on a mystery weekend away, causing Lee to become suspicious of his motives. He concludes Guy must be a gangster, but Barbara and Tim believe his paranoia about the subject stems from his own yearning for Lucys affections

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin is horrified when Ann decides she needs to broaden her horizons by getting a job. Comedy, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Wilton and Peter Egan

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: When Alma discovers that her care-leaving background can help secure her an audition to join a theatre company for a national tour, she jumps at the chance. However, when social services sends through her records, Alma comes face to face with the stark reality of her childhood growing up in and out of the care system. Could this bombshell lead to Alma missing out on an opportunity shes been waiting her whole life for? Comedy, written by and starring Sophie Willan

programme.title: Almas Not Normal
programme.desc: Things are not going well between Lin and Joan and the news that Alma will be leaving for a six-month tour looks like it might push Lin over the edge. When the social worker visits, Lin snaps and disappears, leaving Alma fearing that her mum may have turned back to her old ways

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The staff treat April to a trip to the cinema - but a mix-up with the projectionist spells disaster for the outing, as an old war film is shown instead of the intended movie. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin attends a party being thrown by Paul and seems to find a rapport with another of the guests. However, he is unaware the man is a psychiatrist. Comedy, with Richard Briers

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville faces being left alone at Christmas as all his friends and neighbours make plans to go away. He is particularly dismayed to see Mavis planning a Christmas break with Madge, and terrified to learn that Mr Newbold is plotting an escape from Mrs Featherstones advances, and leaving Granville entirely at her mercy. To make matters worse, the wily shopkeeper has a consignment of dodgy Christmas crackers to sell and a distinct shortage of customers. David Jason stars in the special festive episode

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Bryan welcomes his mail-order bride into his life - and his caravan - but there isnt enough room for her family as well. Llewellyns student helpers disappear, so he hatches a plan to appropriate Boycies staff and the new arrivals in one fell swoop. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grainger is given a temporary promotion and promptly fires Mrs Slocombe - a decision he soon comes to regret. Department store comedy, starring Mollie Sugden and Arthur Brough

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Hobbo discovers that an infirm friend needs help to bury his pet in the churchyard, and calls on Alvin and Entwistle to carry out the deed. However, the mission becomes complicated when the police begin to suspect the trio are up to no good. Comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Burt Kwouk and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Behind Every Bush There Is Not Necessarily a Howard
programme.desc: Hobbo enlists his friends in a scheme to help Toby win back his ex-wife - but finds she only has eyes for Morton. Meanwhile, Howard tries to keep Pearl from interfering with his bike ride with Marina, and Cooper and Walsh test a new piece of in-car technology. Long-running rural comedy, starring Russ Abbot, Trevor Bannister, Peter Sallis and Burt Kwouk

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin attends a party being thrown by Paul and seems to find a rapport with another of the guests. However, he is unaware the man is a psychiatrist. Comedy, with Richard Briers

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville tries to stop Madge from moving away and taking Mavis with her - but there is only one person who can change their minds, and he has been dead for years. He also finds himself with a consignment of incredibly sour crab-apple jelly to get rid of. Mrs Featherstone makes Mr Newbold an offer he cannot refuse, and Eric and Cyril vow to be more careful with their money

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth cannot suppress her excitement when she hears Emmet is rehearsing an amateur production of The Boyfriend, and resolves to land herself a part in the show. Suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and David Griffin

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Bryan welcomes his mail-order bride into his life - and his caravan - but there isnt enough room for her family as well. programme.title: Dads Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.sub-title: The Loneliness of the Long Distance Walker
programme.desc: Remake of the classic sitcom, featuring scripts associated with missing episodes performed by a new cast. Private Walker, source of the platoons supplies of whisky, cigarettes and other essential black-market goods, is called up to serve in the regular Army, so Mainwaring decides to use all his standing as an officer and a bank manager to have him excused from military service. Meanwhile, Jones and the others try to ensure that Walker fails his medical. Kevin McNally stars as Captain Mainwaring, with Robert Bathurst as Sergeant Wilson, Kevin Eldon as Corporal Jones and Mathew Horne as Private Walker
